description:
Conservation Kōrero: Underwater Forests of Wellington, Towering seaweed forests and colourful sponge gardens lie just beneath the surface of Wellington’s coastline. Like forests on land, these underwater habitats support a remarkable diversity of life, including treasured kaimoana species. Yet they face growing pressure from human activity and environmental change.
Dr Nicole Miller will take us on a visual journey through Wellington’s Blue Belt, exploring current conservation successes and challenges. Drawing on years of diving, photography and local restoration work, Nicole will highlight the beauty and fragility of these ecosystems and why it’s more important than ever to bring changes to the surface.
This event will be held in the Pāteke Room in the Visitor Centre with light refreshments provided. Doors open at 6:00pm, parking is available at both our Visitor Centre and overflow parking area on Birdwood Street.
Nicole’s seaweed artworks will be on display at Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne from 1 August to 30 September, with all profits supporting Explore Your Coast: www.exploreyourcoast.co.nz/docu.
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Visit the Upper Hutt Blockhouse (8th & 9th Nov 2025), Visit the Upper Hutt Blockhouse (8th & 9th Nov 2025)
This Heritage New Zealand Pouhere Taonga property is not open to the public often, so bring the family between 11am and 1pm.

About this Event

This Heritage New Zealand Pouhere Taonga property is not open to the public often, so bring the family between 11am and 1pm!
Come visit the Upper Hutt Blockhouse between 11am-1pm as part of the Wellington Heritage Festival 2025.
"The Upper Hutt Blockhouse is one of the few remaining buildings associated with the New Zealand wars, as well as a monument to Pākehā settler anxiety, built in fear of an attack by Māori that never came. Visit today and you’ll get a sense of the defensive ‘last stand’ mindset. On both floors you’ll find a series of balistraria, tightly angled gun slots through which members of the militia would defend their posts. Keep an eye out, too, for an exposed panel where you can see the fine gravel that was packed between the exterior weatherboards and inch-thick interior boards to absorb the impact of answering gunfire. Don’t be fooled by the windows – they were added later. Originally, the loop holes you’ll spy on the walls were the only way to see out."
Inside the Blockhouse, there’s an exhibition from Whirinaki Whare Taonga, telling the early story of Upper Hutt and its Blockhouse.
We will have some knowledgeable people available to share stories, answer questions and some light refreshments. Drop in between 11-1pm and stay as long as you like!

description:
Intro to 2D Traditional Animation - Creative Arts Holiday Programme, This is a beginners course in 2D animation where tweens & teens will learn the time-honoured principles of hand-drawn animation and how to apply them to your own animation project. You may work on a project incorporating your own ideas, or if you'd rather, an assigned a project from your tutor.

You can use any device, ipad or laptop you already own (except a mobile – it's really not big enough to do good animation on). The school does not provide any digital equipment.

Your tutor, Ross Payne, is a 30 year veteran of traditional 2D animation, and loves to share his knowledge about it for you to love it as much as he does.

In each lesson there will be examples and demonstrations of the animation principles with follow up exercises to put your new skills into practice. You'll work on your project both during class and at home, applying the animation principles to your individualised project.

You can choose to draw your animation on paper then scan/capture into your device, or choose to draw your animation digitally, directly into an animation programme. which ever you choose, instructions in class will be provided to give you the skills needed for this step.

Bring a water bottle and snack for morning tea.

This workshop is for ages 10 and above. Tuesday - Friday, 30 September - 3 October, 10 am - 1 pm daily.

This workshop is part of Inverlochy Art School's September School Holiday Programme. For more teen & tween workshops including photography, jewellery making, pottery and sewing head to the 'Tamariki and Teens' section of our website.

description:
Annual Exhibition 2025, Annual Exhibition 2025
Annual Exhibition 2025
The exhibition is being held this year at the Thistle Hall in Cuba St and will run from 29 September to the 5th of October.

Our annual exhibition is the opportunity to showcase the best work of our members to family, friends and the public and we try to include work from each member.

Current members of WPS are invited to submit up to three images to be considered for selection for the exhibition.

Entry
Each member can submit up to three images.
Entries should be sent to by midnight, 30 August 2025.
File Specs: Full resolution under 12MB
File naming: First name, Last Name, Title (if any)
Colour Mode: RGB colour mode (not Grayscale)
Bit Depth: 8 bits/Channel
Colour Space: SRGB
JPEG Format: Baseline (“Standard”)

File Naming
To avoid mixing up each member’s files, each image should have a clear file naming structure. It just allows us to keep better track of the images.
- Number_FirstName_Surname_Title.jpg

Printing and matting
The exhibition will be shown in the club frames, which are 16 x 20 inches. We will arrange printing and mounting of selected images on your behalf. You may elect to print and matt your own images. If this is the case, Please contact us to discuss.

Exhibition dates and times
Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba Street, Te Aro
29 September – 5th October 2025

description:
Wairarapa Walking Festival 2025: StoryWalk®, Turn reading into an adventure with your tamariki at this special StoryWalk® in Carrington Park, Carterton. A StoryWalk® transforms a children’s book into an outdoor journey—pages are displayed on stands along the path, and children can walk, run, hop, skip, jump, or be wheeled to each one as you read together. The StoryWalk® Wairarapa project was instigated by and is managed by Wairarapa Library Service.

Guiding this experience is local Speech Language Therapist Polly Newton, who will share simple, practical tips to help strengthen children’s speech, language, and literacy skills through the way you read with them.

The StoryWalk® is located on a flat, sealed path around the park, making it ideal for prams, wheelchairs, and scooters. Meet by the playground and enjoy a relaxed, family-friendly activity designed for children and their caregivers.

This event is part of the Wairarapa Walking Festival and is proudly supported by REAP Wairarapa, Chatterbox Wairarapa, and Speech Teacher. The StoryWalk® Project was created by Anne Ferguson in collaboration with the Kellogg-Hubbard Library, Montpelier, Vermont, and is a registered service mark owned by the Kellogg-Hubbard Library.

description:
Queensgate Night Market, What started as a humble food market in a suburban Auckland carpark has grown into one of New Zealand’s most popular evening destinations. The Auckland Night Markets bring together street food, community, and live entertainment - and from the 27th September 2025, Queensgate Night Market will be joining the family!
Our market offers a vibrant atmosphere, showcasing an ever-evolving mix of global cuisines, handmade goods, and live entertainment - from buskers to magicians and cultural performers. It’s this unique blend of flavour, fun, and family-friendly energy that’s helped us become a staple in the weekday and weekend plans of thousands of Kiwis.

description:
Balfolk Class - Newtown, Balfolk Class - Newtown
Balfolk is a folk dance and music movement from Europe that gives new life to traditional dances. A ‘Bal Folk’ is a social dance event that brings people of all ages and walks of life together through group and partner dances to have fun and connect. It's a friendly, social and energising space.

The class will run over 6 weeks. We will learn and practice different group and partner dances you would commonly find in a Bal Folk.
Dances are simple and easy to learn but also have depth so once you have learnt the basics, we can explore and play around with variations.
Balfolk is accessible to people with all skill levels. You don’t need any previous dance experience, curiosity is all you need 😊

When: Wednesdays from 6:00 to 7:30pm

Location: Newtown Community Centre

Price: $60 for the block of 6 classes
$15/class for casual attendance
Students/concession/unwaged: koha
label: Classes & Workshops

description:
Āhuru Mōwai Trust Low Sensory Session, Āhuru Mōwai Trust Low Sensory Sessions are held weekly at Chipmunks Playland Tawa.
Session Details:
When: Tuesdays (Yes, even in the school holidays!)
Time: 5:30-8:00pm
Cost: $10 per family (immediate, not extended)
Who: children/young adults with special needs and their parents, guardians, family members, carers, siblings.
On Tuesday's we also host a Brick Therapy session with Dan from Brick Engaged and there is an additional activity fee of $5 per child to participate in that activity.
If you are looking to have a birthday celebration at one of these sessions please txt Paul on 0272787087, or message us in advance. We would also encourage you to make use of a party room to host additional guests (an extra charge would apply to guests)
Come along in any dynamic that suits the family, to these relaxed and open sessions for families and kids living with special needs of all kinds.
Although we cannot cater to everyone's needs, we will do our best to support families to overcome obstacles to attend the sensory sessions.
Please speak with trustees regarding concerns.
(I.D or needs verification may be required at Trustee discretion).
Disclaimer: WE WILL GENTLY AND KINDLY TURN AWAY ANYONE WE FEEL SHOULD NOT BE THERE, AND WE RESERVE THE RIGHT TO DO SO.

The New Zealand Hapkido Federation in association with the Upper Hutt Martial Arts Adacemy is hosting this biennial event.

The 4th Oceania Cup Friendship Tournament will feature Hapkido seminars by Grandmaster Scott Seo from the World Kido Federation / Hanminjok Hapkido Federation, a Masters demonstration, and a Hapkido tournament featuring a range of competition divisions for all ages, 5 and up.

While this event is aimed at Hapkido practitioners, the seminars are open to any interested martial artists and we will consider tournament entries from other martial arts styles. Bear in mind that this is a Hapkido event run under Hapkido rules.

description:
The Annual Celebration of Monty Python Day!, The Annual Celebration of Monty Python Day!
And now for something completely different…
Prepare yourself for a day of absurd antics, ridiculous competitions, and nonsensical fun as The Bond Store descends into Monty Python–inspired mayhem.

💰 Entry is FREE!
But to join the day’s silliness, games, and glorious nonsense, we will be asking for a donation to Kāpiti Golf for Good – a Lions & Rotary initiative.

👑 Gather your knights, twits, and lumberjacks for:

🕺 The Ministry of Silly Walks Competition

🩸 The Black Knight’s Limb Toss (just a flesh wound)

🎩 The Upper-Class Twit of the Year Race

🐴 The Two-Person Coconut Horse Race

🌲 The Lumberjack Song Karaoke (he’s okay!)

🧠 The Monty Python Quiz (bring your brain, or a dead parrot)

🏆 Prizes to be won, dignity to be lost.

But that’s not all! Beware of French guards at the gate, pop into the Argument Tent (“This isn’t an argument!”), and expect confusion, silliness, and possibly a Spanish Inquisition (though nobody expects that).

Plus: Market stalls and Monty Python–themed drinks at the bar.

Plus Steve Poulton live music from 4.30pm.

So grab your coconuts and gallop to The Bond Store for a day you won’t forget (no matter how hard you try).

Kindly stuff your pockets with good old-fashioned cash (none of that funny monopoly money) to hurl generously at the ‘Golf is Good’ charity in exchange for your entry into the day’s utterly ridiculous games

description:
Ruamahanga Farm learning day, At this Toru Trail, we look forward to a day of planting, learning and creativity at Ruamāhanga Farm. Just outside Martinborough, Ruamahanga Farm Foundation are bringing the local community together to help restore wetlands and provide greater access to the awa. The restoration is a foundation for Te Reo o te Wai, an arts and conservation programme for local primary schools.


Our hosts Jane Riddiford and Rod Sugden will share the story of the land and the conversion of a traditional stock and crop farm into a regenerative, community oriented venture. They will involve us in some of the experiential ways they are helping children and young people connect to hidden places along the Ruamāhanga. The day will include planting trees and grasses along a new wetland walk combined with nature based story telling, art and creative writing. We will have a shared lunch by the awa or under canvas if it is wet.
label: Conservation & Environment

description:
Oh The Horror! Spooky Halloween Craft, Trinket and Art Market (All Ages), Welcome to Oh! The Horror - A Spooky Halloween Market For All Ages!
Date: October 26th, 2025
Time: 10:00 AM
Venue: Ngaio Town Hall, 1 Ottawa Road, Ngaio, Wellington 6035, New Zealand.
Calling all fans of the macabre and spooktacular! Get ready for a wickedly delightful experience at our Halloween market. From lollies for big and little monsters to an eerie assortment of wares, this event is a must-visit for all ages.
Dive into the underworld of horror with a tantalizing selection of items such as records, art illustrations, plushies, jewelry, horror DVDs, and more. Whether you're into face painting or fascinated by taxidermy and crystals, this market has something to invoke the dark side within.
Immerse yourself in the chilling ambiance as Mistress Vinyella from On The Record spins a delightfully moody playlist featuring iconic bands like Joy Division, Bauhaus, Sisters of Mercy, A Certain Ratio, and The Cramps. Let the music of the night guide you through the shadows of Halloween.
Embrace the spirit of the season and explore the Gothic decorations, get a flash tattoo, or discover unique pieces to add a touch of darkness to your collection. Join us for a hauntingly good time in honor of the spookiest night of the year!

There are games suited to all levels, and info sheets to help beginners. Playing games is a fantastic way to pick up new kupu and phrases and use what you have already learned.

Kēmu include: Kaupapa, Tākaro!, Kāri Māori, Wero, Whare Whare, Kupu Rorirori, Ko Wai Koe? and any others you bring along.

description:
Manu Park Site Viewing, Manu Park Site Viewing
Meet us and find out how you can build your dream home in a new subdivision that combines outstanding natural beauty and a real sense of community.
The agent is on-site at the end of Karuhiruhi Street every Sunday between 10.00am - 11.00am.
Since its establishment in 2018, Manu Park has become a highly sought-after community, with Stages One and Two fully sold out. Don’t miss your chance to secure a piece of this paradise in Stage 3, where only 8 sections remain.
Manu Park isn’t just a place to live—it’s a place to belong. Designed with families in mind, it offers a safe and welcoming environment where kids can ride their bikes down quiet streets, explore nature on their doorstep, and grow up knowing their neighbours. Centrally located near Waikanae township it offers expansive open spaces and a strong sense of community; Manu Park is a neighbourhood where friendships flourish and families thrive.
Nestled within protected native bush, Manu Park offers breathtaking surroundings, generously sized sections, and the opportunity to build a home tailored to your vision and lifestyle. Named after the Māori word for bird, it sits beside the renowned Nga Manu Nature Reserve, close to Waikanae’s excellent schools and amenities, just minutes from the beach, and under an hour from Wellington city.
Teaming up with local building companies, Manu Park offers House and Land packages for a seamless home-building experience. Visit Manu Park to explore Design and Build opportunities to create a home that perfectly fits your lifestyle.
Don’t miss this rare opportunity to be part of a vibrant community where nature and modern living come together. Visit manupark.nz to learn more and secure your section today

description:
Kāpiti Run for Youth 2026, Kāpiti Run for Youth 2026
We’ve checked the tide timetable – Anchor your calendar now to take part in the 2026 Web Genius Kāpiti Run for Youth on Sunday 29 March 2026 – Raising funds for rangatahi in Kāpiti since 2011 while having fun strolling or running along the beautiful Kāpiti coastline. Kāpiti Run for Youth is a lively, family-friendly event focused on fostering community spirit and supporting organisations that encourage and uplift local young people. RAIN or SHINE, get ready to have fun!

We know Kāpiti Run for Youth helps to support amazing community work done by local fundraising partners. We have 13 incredible fundraising partners you can choose to support and raise funds for. We are very proud of them all and the work they do to uplift the youth of Kāpiti. Each partner brings something unique to the table, from providing essential resources and mentorship to creating spaces where young people can thrive. By supporting rangatahi through education, creativity, employment, and well-being initiatives, they ensure our youth are not just supported but empowered to reach their full potential. Our partners are creating a brighter tomorrow for the young people of Kāpiti!

In the beginning Kāpiti Run for Youth was created by Web Genius as their way as a local business to ‘give back’ to the community so, more than a decade on we can proudly say the event is sustainable and is popular with all sorts, young and old, the fit and not so fit. Kāpiti Run for Youth has raised $280,003 for rangatahi since inception in 2011. In 2025 the event raised $25,531 which was remarkable.

Kāpiti Run for Youth will be open for sign up early in January 2026 >> https://www.kapitirunforyouth.co.nz/signup

At that time, we will be seeking individuals and teams to get together to walk or run from Paraparaumu Beach (Maclean Park) to Raumati Beach and back. For those choosing the 12km walk or run, they will do two circuits of the course.

Choose your adventure – a 6km or 12km run or walk along the beautiful Kāpiti coastline. Whether it's a leisurely stroll or a mad dash to be the first across the finish, you choose the event that suits you best, and which of our amazing fundraising partners you're going to raise funds for. On race day grab your dog and your mates and head down to the beach for fun, family and fitness. Registration takes place from 8.30am to 9.45am with a briefing at 9.45am and all walkers and runners starting together at 10am.

Once your profile is set-up online, let your family, friends and work mates know so they can sponsor your efforts. We encourage every participant to raise $20 through sponsorship instead of paying an entry fee. All entrants receive regular campaigns to motivate their sponsorship efforts along the way.

Kāpiti Run for Youth prides itself on being a sustainable, family-friendly event that is all about supporting each other and having fun. Join your community for a feel-good morning on the beach. Your fundraising makes a difference in the lives of our young people! BYO sunhat, sunscreen, and water on the day, and there are sponsored spot prizes, treats for the kids, certificates for participating and free Paekākāriki Mini Pops for everyone at the end.

Checkout our FAQs over on our website >> https://www.kapitirunforyouth.co.nz/faqs

Closer to the event, go to our website to sign up or sponsor someone that you know who is participating >>
https://www.kapitirunforyouth.co.nz/search

All registrations and sponsorship are handled securely and confidentially on the Kāpiti Run for Youth website that is powered by Raisley and supported by Stripe online payments.

---------------------------------------------

BUSINESS SPONSORSHIP
Platinum $1,000+, Gold $500, Silver $250, Bronze $125 is available for businesses who want to support the underlying work of this event and rangatahi youth of Kāpiti. In 2025 we were grateful to 58 local business sponsors to the value of $15,125 that can be viewed on our website >> https://www.kapitirunforyouth.co.nz/sponsors

Sponsorship for the 2026 campaign will open late in January 2026

---------------------------------------------

FUNDRAISING MODEL
We focus on returning as much money as we can back to the Kāpiti community to those working with rangatahi. All funds raised are split 70% to the official Fundraising Partner chosen by the participant at the time of registration and 30% is retained towards running and promotion of the event.

FUNDRAISING PARTNERS
Birthright Kāpiti, Challenge for Change Kāpiti, Kāpiti Basketball Association, Kāpiti College, Kāpiti Kindness Trust, Kāpiti Youth Support, No 49 Squadron Air Cadets Kāpiti, Ōtaki College, Paraparaumu College, The Shed Project Kāpiti, Whirlwind Kāpiti, Work Ready Kāpiti, ZEAL Kāpiti >>
https://www.kapitirunforyouth.co.nz/fundraising-partners

---------------------------------------------

WELLNESS UPDATE
We operate within the Event Sector Voluntary Code. Please don’t attend if you are feeling unwell.

REGISTRATION DISCLAIMER
Kāpiti Run for Youth carries comprehensive Public Liability event insurance for a period of 48 hours that includes set-up, pack-down and during the event. We maintain an Accident & Incident Register at the Registration Gazebo and local GP, Paraparaumu Medical Centre, handle any medical emergency on the day of the event. We dial 111 if there is an incident beyond their resources.

We remind participants to take self-responsibility and those they are with when on the beach course and to alert officials as needed. It is important to listen to all instructions given on the day of the event at the pre-course briefing, and to report any incidents however minor they may seem. To report an incident, dial 027 244 9585.

PHOTOGRAPHY DISCLAIMER
Kāpiti Run for Youth reserves the right to use any image captured by our official photographer for promotional purposes in any media, in perpetuity without compensation, unless not consented.

description:
Wellington Foraging for Wild Edible Weeds Workshop, Join Felicity of Wild Botanica for a wander around the edges of the Innermost Community gardens to learn about commonly found, wild, edible weeds. Build a relationship with a natural world that is overflowing with abundant, wild, edible, nutritious, medicinal plants. Learn their names & get to know them, along with folklore & stories from the past.

By the end of the workshop you will:
- understand the basic principles of foraging, including sustainable harvesting
- feel confident in identifying 10-15 wild edible plants
- know about their nutritional & medicinal benefits
- know about common toxic/dangerous plants to avoid when foraging
- know about the best ways to prepare your foraged greens for maximum nutritional benefit & flavor
- feel empowered to include foraged greens in your everyday diet

Walk away with hands on, practical skills & knowledge that you can use in your own life.

Investment includes hands on wild weed walk & discussion, cup of foraged herbal tea & wildly delicious foraged snack.

To learn more about Felicity and her offerings,
visit @wildbotanica.nz on social media
or www.wildbotanica.co.nz.
For any queries, please email felicity@wildbotanica.co.nz.

Science Bites School Holidays Science Workshops, Get hands-on with some tasty, edible science these school holidays! Science Bites is an edible science workshop for tamariki aged 8-12 years old.

Learn about rainbow diffraction chocolate, make your own gummy worms, explore some glow-in-the-dark jelly, and more! All the projects in this workshop are safe to eat, meaning kids can taste their creations as they learn.

Tamariki will be excited to come home and share their new understanding of how science can be found in our everyday lives, even our kitchens!

Workshops include a complimentary planetarium show.

Tamariki can be dropped-off in the morning and picked-up afterwards; caregivers are not required to stay for the duration of the workshop. Tamariki should bring a packed lunch.

This workshop will involve handling and consuming some known allergens and substances your child may be sensitive to. Please refer to the list on the Space Place website.

description:
Musos Club Video Premiere, 
Get your best gig gear on and join indie-pop artist Hannah Virk for the premiere screening of the Musos Club music video from her sophomore album Pocket Clocks, including an exclusive live performance, behind the scenes stories and more.
Hannah Virk is an independent musician based in Wellington, New Zealand exploring music on guitar, ukulele and keyboard. Hannah uses her own beats and layers instruments and vocals to create upbeat and captivating songs. Hannah's songs bring together electronic, folk and pop styles to create a truly unique flavour. Described as both quirky and like poetry to music, Hannah's songs are set apart by their vivid imagery and explorative sounds, surprising listeners with every note and melody.
Musos Club is the first single from Hannah's sophomore album Pocket Clocks. The album is an exciting step for Hannah - who began here musical career as an independent artist and producer after getting a guitar at age 30 and letting her creativity bloom. Musos Club dives into the live music scene and her experiences growing up around 'the cool kids' before she started her own musical journey and found her 'wild and free' pathway.
You can find Hannah's music here: https://linktr.ee/hannahvirk
Music video credit: Kobenaboo Productions: @kobenabooproductions2912

description:
Wairarapa Walking Festival 2025: Homewood Airstrip| Roast by the Coast, Enjoy a day out on the Wairarapa Coast with a family-friendly walk and a hearty roast lunch. Situated on working farmland, the Homewood Airstrip walk is a 5km return track that follows a gradual incline to the top, offering sweeping views of land and sea, before an easy downhill return. The gravel track is usually straightforward, but can be slippery or muddy in wet conditions—good footwear and wet-weather gear are recommended. Gates will be open and stock fenced off for the day. Thanks to Tranzit sponsorship the bus fare is significantly reduced for participants.

After your walk, return to the Homewood Storeroom to refuel with a traditional roast lunch of slow-cooked lamb, crispy roast potatoes, and seasonal salad, with an optional slice for dessert.

Walk Format
Travel: 1 hour each way from Masterton (2 hours total)
Arrive: Café at 10am (time for the loo and to collect maps—navigation support by Gavin, our seasoned guide!)
Walk: Depart up the airstrip by 10.30am; allow 2 hours including time to enjoy the views
Lunch: Return to the Storeroom by 12.30pm for a roast lunch
Return: Bus departs from the café at 2pm

This outing is ideal for fit trampers, casual walkers, families, pre-schoolers, and school groups (minimum age 4+). Expect moderate hills and potentially muddy conditions if it rains—but with the right gear, light rain and wind will only add to the adventure.

description:
Mangaroa Farms Open Day 2025, Mangaroa Farms Open Day 2025
Join us on Saturday 21st March from 10am–3pm for our Harvest Festival - a special launch event in partnership with Upper Hutt Food Week.

It’ll be a little different from our usual Open Days: more stalls, more food, and more of our community involved. Expect fresh local produce, regenerative farm tours, kai tastings, nature play for tamariki, a Pumpkin Competition, and more.

🎟️ Tickets are FREE - Please register at https://store.mangaroa.org/products/mangaroa-farms-harvest-festival-sat-21st-march-2026-10am-3pm


HARVEST FESTIVAL ACTIVITIES INCLUDE:
- Local Mangaroa Lunch / BBQ
- Live music & good vibes with Carlos Riegel & Casual Healing
- Garden tours (Very Limited Spaces — Sign Up Now)
- Permaculture Orchard Tours
- Kids Zone with face paint & craft activities
- Local vendors & marketplace
- Coffee cart
- Sheep dog demos
- Sheep shearing
- Farm Trail Loop


ABOUT FOOD WEEK

Upper Hutt Food Week is coming to life for the first time, 21st–29th March 2026 - a celebration of local food, culture, and community. Think of it like the Upper Hutt Arts Trail, but with kai.

All across the region, organisations, cafés, foodbanks, schools, community groups and marae will be putting on a rich spread of workshops, shared meals, foraging walks and events. We’re proud to be kicking it all off at Mangaroa Farms.

It’s a moment to mark the changing of the seasons, celebrate the harvest, and deepen our connection to kai grown here in Upper Hutt. Whether you’re a grower, a cook, a food lover or just curious, this is your chance to meet others who care about where our food comes from and where it could go next.

Then from 22nd–29th March, Mangaroa will continue to host a range of food-focused workshops and experiences across the week - with some wonderful guest facilitators. Details to come.

Come be part of this growing movement toward local food resilience, shared learning and community connection. Nau mai, haere mai. Let’s get amongst it.

📍 Hosted at the Mangaroa Farm Shop / Kete Kai 98 Whitemans Valley Road

Watch this video from our 2024 Open Day: https://vimeo.com/1014588536

description:
Wellington Pasifika Festival 2026 🌺, Wellington Pasifika Festival 2026 🌺
Experience the sights, sounds, and flavours of the Pacific at Wellington Pasifika Festival 2026 🌸🌴🥥

Come along to this free, whānau-friendly festival and join us to celebrate our unique Pacific communities. From art and language to performances and food, the Pasifika Festival is all about acknowledging the richness of our Pacific brothers and sisters.

📅Saturday 7 February 2026, at Waitangi Park, Wellington
⌚12pm-6pm for the main event. Come from 1:30am for the opening ceremony.
📍Waitangi Park, Wellington Waterfront

The 2026 event theme is ‘Arrival’, which will be represented through explorations of migration stories and is a celebration of and a welcome to the Pasifika communities who have made New Zealand their home. You can also view the carving has been taking shape since the 2024 festival and talk to artist Leo Semau about the artwork.

Plus... The popular Pasifika Cuisine Cook-Off competition is back for another year, with food stalls submitting their best dish for judging by a celebrity panel.

The festival will open with a blessing followed by stage performances from various Pacific Island groups. There will be food stalls, craft, and sponsor stalls, a Kids’ Zone, and Pacific Media Network 531pi FM and Āti Awa Toa radio activations.

You can also take part in a festival wide activation based on the card game ‘Lalaga’, designed by Torisse Lalau, which uses Pasifika wisdom (talanoa) to foster empathy and connection through sharing stories.

We’d like to thank the Pasifika Patrol who will be helping out on the day. Gather your friends and family for a fun day out–be transported to the Pacific Islands for the day! ❣️

To see the full programme, head to 👉 https://wellington.govt.nz/news-and-events/events-and-festivals/pasifika-festival

description:
Colossal Squid: Freaky Features, Colossal Squid: Freaky Features comes to Cobblestones, Greytown

The ever-popular Te Papa exhibition Colossal Squid Ngu Tipua opens at Cobblestones on 11th December 2025 and runs through until 1st February 2026.

The Colossal Squid is so rarely seen that only eight adult colossal squid have ever been scientifically reported. Now venue name, in co-operation with Te Papa, is set to reveal some of the fascinating science and wonderful secrets of one of New Zealand’s most significant natural history specimens.

Te Papa's Head of Natural History, Phil Edgar, said, “In learning about this weird and mysterious creature, we hope visitors will have a greater appreciation for life in the deep sea, much of which remains undiscovered.”

The Colossal Squid is one of the most iconic specimens at Te Papa, and while touring the complete specimen is impossible, some of the important parts of the squid have found their way to Cobblestones.
Te Papa’s Curator of Invertebrates, Dr Rodrigo Salvador, explains how important the Colossal Squid has been for the science community, and how popular it has been among the public.

“Te Papa’s Colossal Squid has been a major source of information for scientists and the museum’s most popular exhibit since its debut. They live in the deep Antarctic waters, and despite being one of the largest invertebrate species in existence, they are very rarely captured”.

Colossal Squid Ngu Tipua centres around an interactive table displaying many different objects, including the lens from an eye of the Colossal Squid, part of a tentacle, and a complete tiny squid, a miniature of its colossal relative. Touchable rotating models of a squid’s tentacle hook and the Colossal Squid's beak are also on display.

The exhibition also displays two documentaries which show life in the depths of the Southern Ocean, and how the most complete Colossal Squid ever found ended up at Te Papa.

The public can continue the experience and learn beyond the exhibition with a new Te Papa press book for children, Whiti: Colossal Squid of the Deep, and online Te Papa resources, including a free downloadable Colossal Squid Activity Book.

“Thanks to the support of WBS and Te Papa, Cobblestones is delighted to be bringing this fascinating exhibition to Wairarapa during the summer holidays. It’s a must-see exhibition for the young and young at heart to learn about this fascinating creature, its world, and habitat.”

Colossal Squid Ngu Tipua is developed and toured by the Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa.

For further information, please contact:
Cobblestones Museum - 063049687 or cobblestonesmuseumgreytown@gmail.com

description:
Inaugural Dungeons and Flagons at the Western Tavern, Inaugural Dungeons and Flagons at the Western Tavern
Citizens!

We are super-excited to open up the western cities to These fine folks from ForeverGMNZ, finally get some RPGs going around here! Turn up a bit early to get a bit of courage and story in you and these guys will provide everything you need for an adventure!

Make sure you book below so we can keep track of everything and make sure we have enough DMs. If we get enough interest we will be doing this monthly... (and we will sort out an all-ages series starting in March)

From the event:

Every great Dungeons and Dragons adventure begins in a tavern.

Whether you’re a seasoned adventurer or you’ve never rolled a die in your life, this two-hour, beginner-friendly D&D experience is for you!

Turn up solo or with friends and be guided through a fast-paced adventure by a professional Dungeon Master. Choose your hero — a silver-tongued bard, a fire-flinging wizard, a sword-swinging fighter, a sneaky rogue, or something else entirely, with all characters, dice, and rules provided.

Expect laughs, ridiculous moments, and just enough danger to make the beer taste sweeter. Because while you’re swinging swords and slinging spells, the bar will be open with Beer Engine’s excellent selection of craft brews on tap.

No prep. No pressure. Just great beer and a story you’ll still be talking about afterwards.

This is an age 18+ event.

A 2-day event during the school holidays for Year 7-13. Get ready for amazing worship, games, great sermons and connection.

About this Event

We are excited to invite your teenager to be part of our Encounter Summit - a two-day event designed for our next generation, from Year 7 to Year 13, filled with faith, fun, and fellowship. This summit is a powerful opportunity for young people to grow spiritually, encounter God in a fresh way, build lasting friendships, and create unforgettable memories.
We are thrilled to welcome Brodie McDowell, Youth Pastor at Elim Tauranga and the Elim National Youth Leader. Brodie is an inspiring communicator with a passion for reaching young people, and we believe his ministry will have a significant and lasting impact on our next generation.
Dates:
Friday 3 October 2025 - 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM
Saturday 4 October 2025 - 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM
Meals and Snacks
Provided: Snacks and dinner on both days
Not Provided: Lunch (teens can bring their own lunch or purchase from nearby restaurants)
We look forward to seeing what God will do in the lives of our youth during this incredible weekend. Thank you for supporting your teenager’s spiritual growth and community connections.
If you have any questions, please contact us Simba () or Cassie ().

description:
Hiwinui Sunflower Field Open Day, Hiwinui Sunflower Field Open Day
We’re thrilled to welcome you back for our 2026 Sunflower Season 🌻
A breathtaking summer experience just 10 minutes from Palmerston North and Feilding. Hiwinui Sunflower Field is a rare and unforgettable destination open for a limited time only.


🌻 ENTRY (CASH ONLY)
🚫 NO EFTPOS facilities on site
• Adults (13 years +): $10
• Children under 13 years: FREE

🌻 PICK YOUR OWN SUNFLOWERS
• Bring your own secateurs or hire for $15 (includes 4 stems)
• $3 per stem
• $1 from every bloom donated to Arohanui Hospice 💛

🌿 EXTRA ACTIVITIES
• Wander our walking and flower track – a brand new field design for 2026
• Stunning photo opportunities & new photo props
• Plenty for the kids including:
– Giant Swing
– Scavenger Hunt
– Lawn Games
• Meet the farm animals: Dior the pony and Poppy & Rambo the pet sheep
• Fonterra Barrel Train Rides – (Gold coin donation)
• Face Painting – (Extra charge $5 per person)
• Extended picnic area with our Hamptons marquee for shade and shelter
• Food Trucks (opening weekend & Food Truck Fridays)
• Real Fruit Ice Cream (opening weekend & Food Truck Fridays)

🕒 OPENING HOURS
📅 Saturday 24 & Sunday 25 January
⏰ 10:00am – 4:00pm

🌻 PRIVATE FIELD HIRE
The sunflower field is available for private hire from 5:30pm – 8:30pm.
Limited spaces available — bookings must be made directly.

📍 LOCATION & PARKING
465 Ashhurst Road, Palmerston North
• Located off the main road — please indicate well in advance
• Follow signage to the main car park
• 🚫 Do not enter via the main entrance of Hiwinui Country Estate
(This is EXIT ONLY)

⚠️ HEALTH & SAFETY
As we are a working farm, please note:
• Leave animals at home (except service dogs)
• Wear suitable footwear — tracks may be uneven
• There are bees and pollen in the field — bring medication if required
• Basic first aid is available at the site office

🌻 We can’t wait to welcome you for another unforgettable sunflower season at Hiwinui

The Man Whose Mother Was A Pirate - The Musical, The Man Whose Mother was a Pirate – The Musical is a brand-new musical theatre voyage, with songs and sea-shanties crafted by Te Whanganui-a-tara’s own Nino Raphael. Inspired by Margaret Mahy’s much-loved children’s tale and authorised by the author’s estate, this rollicking production breathes new wind and adventure into one of Aotearoa’s favourite stories.

“I would fight off crews of pirates to watch it again.” -Regional News

Landing at the Hannah Playhouse this December, the production marks the debut season of this high-spirited show right here in Wellington. Sure to have the young and young-at-heart singing along, The Man Whose Mother was a Pirate – The Musical promises swashbuckling fun wrapped up with a heartfelt message.

Tickets go on sale this September - don’t miss out on a seat upon the maiden voyage of this sure-fire hit!

“A perfect treat for kids who want an evening of joyous pirate-y entertainment.” -Wellyott

“Rambunctious chaos across the high seas” -Theatreview

description:
Storytelling On Stage, Storytelling On Stage
Do you love storytelling shows like The Moth? Come join the growing art of live non-fiction storytelling. Learn how to find ideas from your life, craft a compelling narrative, and deliver your personal story in a confident and entertaining way.
 Website description:
Live storytelling is an artform unto itself, requiring different skills and techniques from standup comedy or prose writing. In this four-part workshop, you’ll learn:
- Creative writing exercises and daily practices to to find meaningful stories from your life
- Tools for crafting a compelling personal narrative
- Techniques to build suspense, drama, and humour into your stories
- Stagecraft and performance skills to heighten and polish your delivery
By the end of the class you'll have
- One or more polished 5-10 minute stories to showcase on stage
- Dozens of ideas to expand upon for future stories
- Writing practices to improve your life as a creative
- Less stage fright and more confidence as a speaker
 NOTE: You will be telling true stories from your life in front of the class, building to an (optional) LIVE graduation storytelling showcase at a local venue!
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

description:
Diabetes NZ Nelson Youth: Marlborough Whānau Swim for Diabetes Action Month 2025!, Join Diabetes NZ Nelson Youth for an afternoon at Stadium 2000 in Blenheim.
This family-friendly event is a chance for young people living with diabetes and their whānau to connect, enjoy the pool together, and contribute to our nationwide One Million Minutes of Movement challenge. (Note: this is a public pool session — we’ll be enjoying the space alongside others.)
Come along earlier to support the Blenheim Lions Club “Lap the Map” walk for diabetes awareness — a great way to add even more minutes. Meet at 9am at Raupo Cafe and enjoy a leisurely walk along the Taylor River Reserve.
Registrations (for the swim) are essential and close Thursday, 13 November.
To register or find out more, please contact amanda@diabetes.org.nz.
Bring along:
Togs and towel
Water bottle
Diabetes supplies
Afternoon snack
Please note:
Children 5yrs old and under require an adult swimming with them and to remain within arms reach at all times
Children 9yrs old and under must be actively supervised at all times (watching and able to provide immediate assistance if required)
This is all about spending time together, staying active, and being part of something bigger this Diabetes Action Month.

description:
Friends of Karori Cemetery: Introductory Tour (Feb 2026), Introductory Tour. 
This tour outlines the development of Karori Cemetery since the first burial in August 1891, traversing the earliest burial areas which contain many interesting memorials and monuments. 
Each tour is $10 per person (plus non-refundable booking fee). Children under 12 are free.
Our tour has a maximum capacity of about 15 persons. If there are insufficient tickets remaining for your party, please email us at info@friendsofkaroricemetery.co.nz to check if we can accommodate you.
Please note:
If we have to cancel a tour due to inclement weather or sickness, we will notify you on the day 1 hour before the tour starts by email and on our Facebook page. The booking fee is non-refundable.
This tour is approximately one hour long.
Please dress appropriately for the conditions. While we do our best to guide you on a safe walking route, during the winter months the ground can become slippery in places.
Some of our walks require a moderate degree of fitness and are not suited for those with limited mobility.
There is only one toilet which is located by the cemetery office at the main entrance.
If you have purchased tickets for other people can you please pass our tour information on to them as well.
Please meet us at the shelter (shown as the Historic Chapel on the map at the main entrance)
We look forward to welcoming you to Karori Cemetery.

description:
Fur Patrol, Banished Music is ecstatic to announce the return of one of Aotearoa’s all-time favourite bands, with Fur Patrol returning to the stage for a very special run of shows in March 2026, their first tour since 2024.
From the moment their debut EP Starlifter landed in 1998 until their swansong album Local Kid arrived in 2008, Pōneke’s Fur Patrol carved out an unforgettable legacy in Aotearoa music. Their debut album Pet dominated the New Zealand Albums Chart for an astonishing thirty weeks, while the era-defining single Lydia famously knocked Destiny’s Child off the number one spot. Across three albums, two EPs and hundreds of live shows, Fur Patrol earned a reputation as one of the country’s most formidable and unmissable live acts.
Now, ahead of this year’s Homegrown Festival, Julia Deans (guitar, vocals), Andrew Bain (bass), and Simon Braxton (drums) are hitting the road once more, joined by longtime collaborator and Pet producer David Long (The Mutton Birds). David brings his inimitable guitar wizardry to weave rich sonic layers through the trio’s unmistakable foundation.
Anyone who’s caught Fur Patrol live in recent years will attest they’ve lost none of their potency, power, or energy. These shows promise a celebration of a timeless catalogue, packed with beloved anthems and delivered with the same fire that made the band legendary.
Don’t miss your chance to see one of Aotearoa’s most beloved bands return to the stage this March.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Mātai Moana / Mount Crawford Historic Walking Tour, Mātai Moana / Mount Crawford Historic Walking Tour
Explore Māori settlements, one of New Zealand's oldest farms, military fortifications, and prison buildings, on a guided walking tour of Mātai Moana, Miramar.

First settled in the 1350s, Mātai Moana / Mount Crawford has a rich and layered history of Māori settlement and conflict, early agriculture, and farming, military fortifications, and changing prison populations.

Today, it is a predator-free paradise for a thriving native wildlife population, occasional filming site, and much-loved recreation space for locals and visitors alike.

Join Kate Curtis, history educator and creator of the Mātai Moana historic trail map, on a guided walking tour full of stories of life up the hill. The tour will begin outside the now-closed Wellington Prison, and end with a cuppa in the historic Miramar Prison Garden.

Children and on-leash dogs are welcome.


Booking is REQUIRED
Cost: suggested koha $20

The Sunday 19 October session is fully booked.
Booking information here: https://wellingtonheritagefestival.co.nz/event/matai-moana-mount-crawford-historic-walking-tour/


Health & Safety
This is a walking tour with some uneven terrain. A reasonable level of fitness is needed. Please wear comfortable shoes and dress for the weather.

Please wear good shoes and dress for the weather. The tour may be cancelled in bad weather.

description:
Choral Connections Concert, NZCF presents Choral Connections, a concert featuring two of New Zealand’s finest youth choirs – the acclaimed New Zealand Secondary Students' Choir, directed by Sue Densem, and the Aotearoa Academy Choir, directed by Rowan Johnston and Elise Bradley.
Set in the soaring acoustics of Wellington’s Cathedral of St Paul, this public concert forms the centrepiece of NZCF’s Choral Connect convention – a bi-annual professional development conference that brings together choral directors from around the country.
At the end of the concert, conference delegates with join with the two youth choirs to form a massed choir of over 150 voices to sing under the direction of international choral clinician Dr Pearl Shankuan.
New Zealand Secondary Students' Choir
Aotearoa's national choir for secondary school students, NZSSC gathers during school holidays to rehearse and perform across the motu. Each two-year cycle culminates in an international tour: in July 2024, the choir travelled to China, where they took part in the Shanghai Oriental Art Centre Chorus Exchange, the Chorus and Conducting Art Conference in Hangzhou, and the China International Chorus Festival in Beijing.
NZSSC is directed by Sue Densem, alongside Assistant Conductor and Accompanist Brent Stewart and Assistant Conductor and Vocal Consultant Ben Madden.
Aotearoa New Zealand Academy Choir
The Academy Choir was established as a pipeline for talented singers from across Aotearoa who are aspiring to join NZSSC or the NZ Youth Choir. In 2024, the Academy Choir received a Gold Diploma at the World Choir Games in Auckland.
The Aotearoa Academy Choir is co-directed by Elise Bradley and Rowan Johnston.
Dr Pearl Shangkuan is a highly sought-after conductor, lecturer and clinician who has led performances and workshops on six continents. She is the National President-elect of the American Choral Directors Association (ACDA), the chorus director of the Grand Rapids Symphony and Professor Emerita of Music at Calvin University in Grand Rapids, Michigan. She has conducted several times at Carnegie Hall and other major concert halls in the US. Her guest conducting engagements regularly take her to Europe, Asia, and across North America.

Wild Coffee Workshop, Wellington, forage, cook and taste + Healthy potluck, Wild Coffee Workshop, Wellington, forage, cook and taste + Healthy potluck
Foraging and Growing Workshops coming to Wellington. No need for prior knowledge we will learn everything from scratch.
I'm hitchhiking all the way from Chch to Whangarei and back to Chch with a purpose to pass on knowledge while visiting my friends.

Workshop description:

Come to enjoy learning over a foraged cuppa with a fun and hands-on workshop all about wild coffee! We’ll head out to forage for plants that can be used as a coffee alternative, then cook, brew, and taste our way through the process together.
You’ll learn how to spot potential coffee subs in nature, prep them from scratch, and sip your way through a few different brews. Tasting is a big part of this one—so come hungry and curious!
No experience needed—just bring your sense of humor
neighbors, friends or come solo💚

_______
Come with your family, friends, neighbors and furry companions, please share this event.
_________
I encourage everyone to walk, cycle or bus to the event as part of the experience and help not to impact the environment. I will have some special gifts for the ones who do 🌻💚
________
Please allow enough time to immerse into the workshop with no rush"
_______
REGISTRATION IS Essential the event is number of registered people dependent.
50$ per person per workshop, 80$ for 2 ppl within a family, children are free, animals welcome. description:
River City Express, Travel to the beautiful riverside town of Whanganui, hauled by both steam locomotive Ab 608 and heritage diesel electric locomotive Da 1410. Choose between doing your own thing or jumping on a serene cruise of the Whanganui River on board the coal-fired paddle steamer "PS Waimarie".

Leaving Kapiti, as most others are still sound asleep, we quickly escape the coast and delve into the rurality of the Horowhenua. After a stop in Palmerston North, we head up the trunk through the townships of Feilding and Marton before turning onto the Marton - New Plymouth railway line for the last stretch into Whanganui. After crossing the 290km long Whanganui River, we proceed slowly down the short 5km branch line taking us to Taupo Quay.

From here, it's up to you what you wish to do!

- Sail On The Queen Of The River - Waimarie
This option includes a river cruise aboard the coal-fired paddle steamer Waimarie, restored after around 50 years submerged. The cruise features commentary on Whanganui’s river history, and you can view the boiler and possibly send a message via homing pigeon. The fare covers return train travel, bus transfers, and the cruise.

Departing Paekakariki/Paraparaumu/Waikanae/Otaki/Levin
Adult 13+ yrs - $209
Child 2-12yrs - $179

Departing Palmerston North/Feilding
Adult 13+ yrs - $189
Child 2-12yrs - $169

An Artisan Lunch for Two - $35 add-on

OR

- Do Your Own Thing & Explore Whanganui
You’ll be transferred by bus to the Riverboat Centre and can explore Whanganui on foot or by hiring a vintage cruiser bike. Attractions include the Durie Hill Elevator, Riverboat Museum, NZ Glassworks, Motua Gardens, and local shops and eateries. A tram ride is also available for a small fee. This option includes train fare and bus transfers.

Fares
Departing Paekakariki/Paraparaumu/Waikanae/Otaki/Levin
Adult 13+ yrs - $159
Child 2-12yrs - $139

Departing Palmerston North/Feilding
Adult 13+ yrs - $139
Child 2-12yrs - $119

-Evening Meal
Sandy and the team at the Station Hotel will be on hand to provide an evening meal at your seat on departure from Marton.
Being served is a tasty chicken salad main, a freshly baked mini baguette, along with a sweet chocolate brownie to cleanse the palate!
Vegans, Vegetarians and gluten-free options available, and if there are any allergies, please let us know when booking!
$30 per person to add this to your booking.

description:
Forest & Bird WALK a New Reserve - Mātai Moana / Watts Peninsula, Join us for a guided walk over Mātai Moana / Watts Peninsula (North end of Miramar Peninsula). This guided walk will celebrate the Government announcement (September 2025), that the 72 hectare area is now a Public Reserve and DOC land.
The area's attractions include:
1. strong historical, cultural and military values, (several kainga and pā, and military history from 1880s), and has hosted several prisons, including a prison garden
2. some superb 360 degree views of Wellington Harbour
3. no major animal pest species and while much of the land is grass or in pines, there are several sizable and growing areas of regenerating native bush.
Moderate fitness will be needed. Our walk is expected to take a little over 2.5 hours. It will traverse a mixture of sealed roads and walking tracks with some uneven ground and two quite steep sections, from sea level to 163m above sea level. One section has a gradient of 30 degrees for a short section. There are some stiles to climb over.
Meet opposite the steps up to Massey Memorial at 130 Massey Road, Point Halwsell, Mapuia, Wellington, 6022. This is about 450m south of Point Halswell, and 500m north of the northern end of Shelly Bay. Location of the meeting point: approx 41°17'15" S, 174°49'26.5" E. A Forest & Bird Flag will be on display. See map for meeting spot and car parking suggestions. Please arrive in time to confirm your attendance and receive a quick pre-walk briefing.
Arrive by 12.50 p.m. on Sunday 22 February 2026. Please register on EventFinda in advance - limited to 40 walkers. All aged 10 or more, are welcome - you do not have to be a Forest & Bird member. All children must be accompanied by an adult who takes responsibility for them. Please bring your own sunhat, sunblock, snacks, any personal medications plus water bottle and wear good walking shoes/boots. There is no public transport to the meeting point.
If weather is wet/unsuitable, a decision to postpone will be made by 11:00 a.m. on Sunday 22 February 2026, and a group email will be issued to all those who have registered. Contact Ken New on ken.new@branch.forestandbird.org.nz for any enquiries.
Please register on EventFinda.

description:
Painting in Oils, This class is open to all levels of students interested in exploring the possibilities of this traditional medium. The main exercises will be based on a still life, however students are free to explore subjects of their choice.

This course focuses on the technique of oil painting. You will learn to

- Choose a colour palette
- Choose and prepare supports on which to paint on with oils
- Create a composition according to the subject selected
- Learn how to render volume and form
- Learn expressive qualities of colour and brushwork
- Students will also learn to stretch their own canvas

If you want to explore the possibilities of painting in oils then this is the course for you.

Eight week course: Tuesdays, 10 Feb- 31 March(8 weeks), 10-1pm
Basic materials are included.
Suitable for ages 16 +

Who knows, we might have a few more new tracks to drop by then. Either way, it’s been a while since we toured, and we’re gagging for it.”

description:
Hannah Virk - Musos Club Video Premiere - NZ Fringe Festival, Get your best gig gear on and join indie-pop artist Hannah Virk for the premiere screening of the Musos Club music video from her sophomore album Pocket Clocks, including an exclusive live performance, behind-the-scenes stories and more.

Hannah Virk is an independent musician based in Wellington, New Zealand, exploring music on guitar, ukulele and keyboard. Hannah uses her own beats and layers instruments and vocals to create upbeat and captivating songs. Hannah's songs bring together electronic, folk and pop styles to create a truly unique flavour. Described as both quirky and like poetry to music, Hannah's songs are set apart by their vivid imagery and explorative sounds, surprising listeners with every note and melody.

Musos Club is the first single from Hannah's sophomore albu,m Pocket Clocks. The album is an exciting step for Hannah, who began her musical career as an independent artist and producer after getting a guitar at age 30 and letting her creativity bloom. Musos Club dives into the live music scene and her experiences growing up around 'the cool kids' before she started her own musical journey and found her 'wild and free' pathway.

You can find Hannah's music here: https://linktr.ee/hannahvirk
Music video credit: Kobenaboo Productions: @kobenabooproductions2912

I Don't Know Much About Art, But I Know What I Lick (development season), I Don't Know Much About Art But I Know What I Lick is a show about fine art for messy people.
The gallery is a space with particular rules of engagement. Young children do not know these rules and embrace art in the way that makes the most sense to them: physically.
In I Don't Know Much About Art But I Know What I Lick, Trick of the Light Theatre (The Bookbinder, The Griegol) join forces with internationally acclaimed performance designer Meg Rollandi on a new show for children (5 and under) and their accompanying adults. Guided by two performer attendants, audiences are invited to explore a gallery set up for kids, featuring satirical and interactive reworkings of iconic art pieces - The Mona Lisa with eyes that actually follow you, the Birth of Venus in a paddling pool clam shell...
Development season at NZ Fringe
10, 12 + 2pm Sat 28 Feb + Sun 1 Mar

Purple Poppy Day honours the animals who served alongside people in times of war, companions whose loyalty and courage are woven into New Zealand’s military history.

At this special ceremony, hear stories remembering courageous service animals and meet Paradox the horse and Kingi the dog, who will join the event to represent the heroes in these stories. Come along to a summer's evening at Pukeahu to acknowledge our service animals.

description:
Porirua Parkrun, What is Porirua parkrun? It is a free 5km walk or run event - It’s not about coming first or winning. You can go at your own pace and just enjoy being with other people. It is inclusive and the perfect way to start your weekend in the outdoors chatting to people.

The start occurs just upstream of the Bothamley Park carpark. This car park is off Champion street, opposite the intersection with Windley Street. Head to end of carpark where the concrete ends and there is a gate. For location see https://goo.gl/maps/wEohfp9bRu82.

The course is following the old coach road (circa 1870's) established for the early farms in the area. Today this historic track is located within a park, away from cars and now used by walkers, runners and cyclists.

The track is generally 3 metres wide with a gentle gradient and a compacted gravel surface beside the Kenepuru Stream through a picturesque wooded wild valley. (Note there is a pinch point for a short section after the 2km marker where participants need to go single file each direction for approx 30 metres)

The valley runs east-west with mature trees which means this has to be one of the most sheltered places, out of the wind to exercise in Wellington.

What does it cost to join in? Nothing - it's free! but please register before your first run or walk, so you can get your timing results. You only need to register with parkrun once, to participate at any of the parkrun events around the world. Don't forget to bring a copy of your barcode. If you forget it, you won't get a time.

How fast do I have to be? Walking is fine as long as you can finish the 5km. Please come along and join in whatever your pace!

One dog per adult is welcome, as long as it is on a short hand held lead.

Children welcome but those 10 years and under need to be kept within arms reach of a supervising adult.

Note there is no toilets in the park, so go before you go.

We're friendly! Every week we grab a post park run/walk coffee/tea at Get Fixed within Kai Tahi at 20 Parumoana Street, to mix exercise with socialising.

There are volunteering opportunities even week. So those doing Duke of Edinburgh or similar challenges can get volunteer credits. Ensure you contact us well in advance at "porirua@parkrun.co.nz"

description:
Under Pressure: How to Thrive in a Hyper-Competitive Youth Sport World, Youth sport today asks more of young people than ever before. Selection starts earlier, competition runs deeper, and pressure moments arrive before many athletes have the skills to cope. When this gap exists, confidence wavers, enjoyment fades, and potential remains untapped.

In this keynote, Dr Craig Harrison reveals what’s really happening under pressure, and what it takes to thrive within it. Drawing on developmental science, lived stories from youth sport, and practical tools, this session shifts the conversation from “handling pressure” to working with it.

First, you’ll learn how the adolescent brain, stress response, and social dynamics influence behaviour under pressure. Then, you’ll discover how to build the skills to meet pressure moment by moment, and create the conditions for confident, adaptable performance.

Who is this talk for?
- Parents of youth athletes wanting to better support their child
- Coaches of youth athletes wanting to develop more resilient players
- Teen athletes wanting to perform under pressure

Bond-Murray Room - Doors open 645 PM for a 7 PM start

About Dr Craig Harrison:
With over 20 years’ experience working with developing athletes, coaches, and families, Craig is one of New Zealand’s leading voices on helping young people thrive in sport. His work blends cutting-edge research in long-term athlete development and child psychology with real-world stories from schools, clubs, and academies around the world.
label: Sports & Fitness

description:
Silver Ring-Making Day, This great one-day class is ideal for beginners, but experienced students are also welcome. Have fun making your own ring or perhaps a wonderful present for a loved one! The workshop runs from 10am - 4pm with a lunch break.

Students will practice making simple textured or polished rings in either copper or brass, then move onto making one or more silver rings from start to finish. They will learn measuring, texturing, soldering and finishing techniques to make beautiful, wearable rings!

Students can choose from creating textured, stamped or polished rings in flat metal, or adding in stacking rings in wire and bar. More experienced students may wish to make a wider ring, or a series of rings, perhaps in a tapered form or including finer details and finishing, or possible stone setting. This is a one-day class aimed at either providing a fun experience with a great result, or excellent grounding in basic jewellery techniques for those wanting to pick up the activity. Course fee includes practice materials and 8g sterling silver. Extra materials are available to purchase in the workshop if time allows. As this is only a one-day class we will be providing a set range of widths for your ring bands so as to make the project enjoyable and achievable.

Kids aged 12 and up (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
label: Classes & Workshops

description:

description:
Kiwi Christmas in Battle Hill, Kiwi Christmas in Battle Hill
🎄🎄Celebrate the season at Battle Hill Regional Park – Christmas carols and movie night in the park 🎄🎄

Gather the whānau and head to Battle Hill Regional Park for a magical evening of festive fun on the farm.

Join us for Nick Tansley’s Pop Up Carols Caravan, bringing joyful live music to the park.

Stay later for a screening of the movie Kiwi Christmas on the big screen—a funny, whānau-friendly movie about Santa turning up for a summer holiday as far away from the North Pole as possible – Aotearoa!

Carols: 5.30pm - 6.30pm

Movie: 6.30pm - 8pm

Warm up with marshmallow toasting and grab a bite from the Riding for the Disabled fundraising food stall (cash only). If conditions allow, join a guided walk to see glow worms later that night.

Why not make a weekend of it? Come camp at the Battle Hill campground and enjoy everything the park has to offer—from swimming holes and forest walks to peaceful picnic spots and farm animals.

Get the whānau involved in Summer Events

Discover the magic of your regional parks. For 25 years, Greater Wellington has been hosting recreational events that help people connect with nature, get active outdoors, and enjoy time together.

Battle Hill Farm Forest Park is more than just a beautiful place—it’s a hub for ecological restoration and history. Greater Wellington’s work here restores native bush, showcases farm animals for children, preserves historic sites, and helps build resilience to climate change. Our mana whenua partners, farmers, and volunteers play a vital role in keeping this park a special place for everyone.

description:
Soirée at the Zoo: A Night for our Whānau & Animals, Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo and Ronald McDonald House Charities® (RMHC®) New Zealand invite you to an exclusive fundraising event: Soirée at the Zoo: A Night for our Whānau & Animals.

Join the two charities for a refined evening of celebration and generosity, where compassion for people and care for wildlife come together in a truly unique setting.

Buy your tickets now!

Guests will experience Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo after hours, enjoy elegant hospitality, and take part in an inspiring night of entertainment and giving, all in support of Wellington families and animals.

What to expect:
- A rare opportunity to explore Wellington Zoo after hours! Join an exclusive Zoo tour and discover the Zoo from a whole new perspective.
- A complimentary welcome drink and delicious canapés throughout the evening
- An engaging live auction and raffle with exceptional prizes
- An entertaining MC, live local music and a chance to dance the night away
- Opportunities to connect with friends, colleagues, and familiar Wellington faces
- The moving story of a local RMHC® NZ whānau
- An insight into Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo’s vital conservation mahi in Aotearoa and around the world

This fundraising event will make a tangible difference to Wellington families and animals. All proceeds from Soirée at the Zoo will be shared equally between Ronald McDonald House Charities® New Zealand and Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo.

Every day, RMHC® NZ provides a home-away-from-home for more than 100 families, offering comfort, care, and stability during difficult times. Your attendance — and your generosity on the night — will directly support families with essential accommodation and meals.

Through their community partnership, Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo welcomes RMHC® NZ families to the Zoo at no cost. Each year, over 400 adults and tamariki visit the Zoo to reconnect with nature and the restorative power of wildlife, joining the 250,000 people annually who experience the Zoo as a place of wellbeing and inspiration.

With only 150 seats available, this will be an unforgettable evening of purpose and celebration.

Secure your place today and be part of a night that supports whānau, wildlife, and the community that connects us all.

Buy your tickets now!

Invoicing options are also available; please email venues@wellingtonzoo.com for more information.

Please note: Event data will be shared with both RMHC® NZ and Te Nukuao Wellington Zoo.

description:
November Full Moon Drumming (10th year anniversary), November Full Moon Drumming (10th year anniversary)
Please remember this is a ZERO WASTE and *NO ALCOHOL* community gathering.

Kia ora!

This upcoming gathering marks our 10th year anniversary. Over 10 years many people from all walks of life have joined and participated, adding to the collective co-creation. Each and every one has made this gathering what it is! We'd like to thank you all and to extend an invitation for a potluck-style birthday celebration from 5pm on Wednesday 5th of November, with Bday cake and lighting the fire at around 7pm. Come along, everyone is welcome!

If this is your first time, make sure to read the full event description bellow:

Full Moon Drumming Wellington is a registered public event with the Wellington City Council. We are a group of humans from all walks of life who gather under the full moon each month to connect through drumming and dance around the fire. This gathering is participatory, that means if you made it to the circle you are part of the circle. As in Africa, there is no audience! Everyone is invited to participate as part of the ‘chorus’ (singing/clapping), dancing, or drumming.
The event is weather permitting, we check the forecast a few days prior and aim to make a decision the day before the event, although this is not possible and sometimes we make the decision on the day. All updates are posted on the event page.
This gathering is entirely run by volunteers, if you'd like to contribute in any way please get in touch.
There are a few things that we need help with:
- FIREWOOD: Please bring dry firewood as a koha, no wood no fire! Please NOTE we DO NOT burn pallets (due to nails) or treated timber. Driftwood from nearby south coast beaches is often abundant, feel free to post or reach out if you have a vehicle and are keen to help gather firewood on the day of the event.
- BEAUTIFYING THE CIRCLE SPACE: Anyone has fairy lights, oil burners, or is keen to make some signs?
NOTE: We hold a fire permit for this event. For safety reasons we don't allow participants to feed the fire, and while is generally okay to roast some marshmallows early in the evening we don't allow the use the fire for cooking or heating food.
This is an **ALCOHOL FREE EVENT**, please help us uphold this kaupapa and remind those who are unaware and might be new to the event. As hosts we are responsible for your safety and we take that responsibility seriously, especially given that the event is authorised through both fire and event permits. Large, public, all ages gatherings require administration and logistics so please respect this and the guidelines provided.
Princess Bay is within Taputeranga Marine Reserve ( https://octopus.org.nz/taputeranga-marine-reserve), we are welcomed to gather each month in this special place so let’s all make sure there is NO RUBBISH LEFT BEHIND and that we take care of the space. Dogs are NOT ALLOWED at the gathering - there are Korora (Blue Pinguins) nesting under the rocks nearby and we've had issues with dogs in the past.
Children are welcome at the fire, though, especially after dark, please make sure that all children have adult supervision, that the supervisors are aware of the children they are looking after, and that the children are aware of who is looking after them.
Feel free to bring:
- Your friends.
- Drums or any percussive instrument.
- Warm clothing; jackets and wool blankets are good.
- Something to seat on (otherwise sand is often wet)
- Drinking water.
- Food, tea to share.
- Your lovely selves.
- Fire spinning kit and fuel.
FIRE SPINNERS: Please take all precautionary safety measures and make sure to set up fire-spinning downwind from the fire circle.
PARKING: There's a parking lot a few hundred meters from the fire circle where there's also public toilets. Please also be aware of our neighbors driveway opposite the stairway and do not park directly in front of it.
CARPOOLING: If you don't have a way to get to Princess Bay, or if you have spare seats in your car, feel free to post it on the event page. The less cars driving the better.
Feel free to share the event page and send an event invite to whomever you feel would be interested, not everyone sees the notifications and advertising
label: Community & Culture

description:
North Pole Express Kapiti: 1pm - Saturday, 20 December 2025, North Pole Express Kapiti: 1pm - Saturday, 20 December 2025
Be whisked away on the North Pole Express to meet Santa, sing, and celebrate this special time of year.

About this Event


Public sales commence 1 September 2025.
Nice List presale: 12pm, 25 June 2025 - 5pm, 1 July 2025. Password required.
No tickets will be held during presale, any remaining tickets will be offered to the public from 12pm on 1 September 2025. To join the 'Nice List' visit the .
If you would like to purchase a private carriage for your group on this service, please email prior to 15 June 2025.



Join The Magical Journey to meet Santa!
Your tickets include:
Entertainers
Carolling
Iced cocoa
Cookies
Souvenir Mug
A special souvenir from Santa
Santa
Extra surprises along the way
Our Candy Class and Claus Class Guests travel in comfort and receive extra treats along the way.


Prepare to be surrounded by the magic and wonder of the Christmas season!
Families will love the magical train ride in one of our specially decorated carriages. You’ll meet Santa, sing, and celebrate this special time of year.
Set to the sounds of carols passengers are invited to join us to be whisked away on the North Pole Express. Delight in the magic of Christmas as the train departs for a journey to collect Santa who has been magically transported to an undisclosed location along the tracks.
From the distance the North Pole Express arrives then the train Conductors invite you and the other passengers to climb aboard shouting “All aboard!”.


All Aboard
During the first leg of the journey wide-eyed children of all ages are invited to engage with characters who are eager to hear what makes the Christmas season special to them.
You’ll meet the conductor, and join in the fun singing and dancing with your hosts and a few other characters who will appear in your carriage on the journey.
At the 'North Pole' Cocoa Class passengers enjoy their iced chocolate from their souvenir mug, and a delicious cookie served by our North Pole Express hosts. Candy and Claus Class passengers will be served aboard the train on the way to the North Pole.
Along the journey Santa appears in your carriage to present each passenger with their very own Christmas ornament. During the trip, characters in each carriage lead passengers in carolling and entertainment as the train makes its way back home.
Children UNDER 2 years (0-23 months) and travelling on the lap of an Adult are FREE. All Passengers 2 years old and over must hold a purchased ticket.

Please note: Seating in our Candy and Claus Classes is strictly limited. Candy and Claus Class Guests receive some extra surprises and treats along the way.
Seating in all carriages is arranged in a configuration of 4 seats as per normal train travel. NPE always endeavour to ensure those who book more than 4 seats are seated in close proximity to each other to ensure they can enjoy the experience together. Children under the age of 2 and travelling on the lap of an adult travel for free.
Wheelchair access: Due to the age and design of the carriages it is not possible to board whist seated in a wheelchair as the entrance is stairs and the doorways are very narrow. However we will be pleased to convey passengers of limited mobility whenever possible provided they can step onto the train from the platform, climbing a step and move to their reserved seats with the help of an able bodied carer. Our crew would assist guiding the passenger up the step onto the train and arrange for the storage of the wheelchair in the guards van. The wheelchair must be able to be folded up and cannot be used on the train. Please let us know when booking if you need wheelchair assistance.

Photo credit: John Bovis

description:
Natural Textured Rings, Natural Textured Rings
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special! :-)

Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!

Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!

Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jewellers torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.

This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.

Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

Workshop fee is $128, early bird $115 till 27 October.

description:

Kicking off at midday in Waitangi Park, you'll find the main stage programme packed with local talent. Enjoy live music, dance, and cultural performances. Dotted around Waitangi Park, you’ll also find a selection of delicious kai , stalls, a Kids’ Zone, a treaty education space, and much more. 
Te Rā o Waitangi 2026 acknowledges tūrangawaewae and whakapapa for all — where is the place you lay down your roots? Where is your family from? In 2026, Waitangi Day celebrates the things that we all share and the things that make us different.
