description:
Beyond the Bookshelves: Diversity in Austen Retellings, Join Rachel Pilois, passionate Austen lover (owner of over 30 copies of Pride and Prejudice) and Children's Book Buyer at Unity Books, for an illuminating exploration of diversity in contemporary Austen retellings. As a mixed-race, queer, and neurodiverse bookseller, Rachel brings a unique perspective to the rich tapestry of Austen-inspired literature.
In this talk, Rachel will showcase a curated selection of titles—all available through Unity Books—that reimagine Austen's beloved stories through diverse lenses. Discover queer retellings, tales set in other cultures, modern adaptations, and even Regency-era stories that amplify marginalized voices.
Attendees will gain a deeper appreciation for the breadth of the current Austen book market and its resonance with the modern world. Prepare to expand your reading list and discover exciting new perspectives on timeless classics.
Part of the Jane Austen Society of Aotearoa New Zealand's 2026 series on diversity.
Bring a friend: purchase 2 or more tickets and get a 10% discount
Who this event is for:
People who love Austen
People who love books
People who care about diversity
Afternoon tea provided

Image: Pride & Prejudice Pride flag by AmateurAusten (available for purchase on multiple items on Red Bubble)
Austen, Maths and Queer nerds unite! A take on Jane Austen's Pride and Prejudice but you don't have to be an Austenite to appreciate the sentiment because Pride truly is Greater than Prejudice.
FAQs
Do I need to know about Jane Austen?
No. Everyone is welcome.
Do I need to be a member of the society?
We don't technically have membership, if you turn up we think of you as a member.
I have accessibility or dietary requirements
We aim to meet at venues that are accessible and provide for all dietary requirements. Please check out the accessibility section or contact us if you're unsure. We want you to be there and we want you to be comfortable.
We aim to provide an accessible and safe environment. Discounts are available for nonwaged members, early birds, volunteers and people buying multiple tickets. Please let us know how we can ensure you're included.

Choose comfortable, flat shoes you don’t mind getting colourful

$5 per packet | 3 for $10 | 6 for $20 | 10 for $30

Come along and celebrate colour, culture and community in the most joyful way possible.

description:
Sera Devcich and Lana Walters present - Two Broke Mums, Sera Devcich and Lana Walters present: Two Broke Mums!

With a strong background in theatre, award winning actress Sera Devcich has turned her stage prowess to the dark art of stand up comedy. Since then she has rapidly climbed up the comedy rank, performing across the country with NZ's top comics.
Sera Devcich is a must see act.

A regular panelist on 7 Days and The Project, you may have also seen her in the 2022 Best Foods Comedy Gala or the Great Comedy Debate.

Lana got her start as a 2016 Raw Come

dy Quest and has gone on to perform her own shows in the New Zealand International Comedy festival

“Lana Walters is a comedic force! Jam packed with top notch jokes from start to finish”

description:
Jewellery Making In 4 Weeks - Sunday Afternoons, This inspiring Sunday afternoon jewellery blocks are the best way for beginners to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment! The course starts on Sun 28 September and runs for 4 weeks until Sun 19 October, 2pm-5pm.

Beginners will practice the basic techniques in copper or brass and then move on quickly to working in silver. An excellent first project for beginners in this class is to create a simple textured ring band - but once you have the basics down, it is fun to create earrings, pendants or other simple items.

Learning to cut, heat, form and texture metal - to solder using a jeweller's torch and silver solder, and finally to polish your work up to a beautiful finish: these classes are super satisfying.

These blocks are also a great way for existing students or more advanced makers to join into a class in our well-equipped warm and bright studio!

Basic materials are supplied and there are extra materials available to purchase as you need in class.
label: Classes & Workshops

description:
Suzanne Vega, Widely regarded as one of the foremost songwriters of her generation, Suzanne Vega emerged as a leading figure of the folk revival of the early 1980s. Four decades later, she is still writing and singing great songs. In her Wellington show, Suzanne will perform a career-spanning set. See her on stage at St James Theatre performing favourites like Tom’s Diner, Luka, Marlene On The Wall, and plenty more. There’ll also be new songs from her 2025 album, ‘Flying with Angels’. Joining her on stage will be longtime guitarist, Gerry Leonard, and cellist Stephanie Winters. 
Since the release of her self-titled, critically acclaimed 1985 debut album, Suzanne Vega has played to sold-out concerts across the world. She is a masterful storyteller who, according to one The New York Times critique, “observes the world with a clinically poetic eye.” Her song lyrics often focus on city life, ordinary people, and real-world subjects. They are notably succinct, understated, and immediately recognisable.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
The King's Singers, The King's Singers
Double-GRAMMY Award-winning vocal ensemble The King’s Singers return to New Zealand following standout performances in 2024 and 2018.

For over half a century, they have represented the gold standard in a cappella singing. Renowned for their unique approach to “close harmony”, they seamlessly weave their voices across centuries and styles.

Spanning Renaissance sacred music and English folksong to jazz standards and pop classics, this programme journeys through their history. From Byrd to Billy Joel, madrigals to The Beach Boys, Disney classics, contemporary works, plus two songs specially selected for Wellington.

Significantly, this concert is one of the last in Aotearoa for baritone Chris Bruerton, a proud New Zealander and key member of the group for over ten years.

Whether you’ve followed them for decades or are hearing them for the first time, Close Harmony at Wellington Cathedral promises an unforgettable night.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Running into the Sun - Te Auaha, Wellington, Running into the Sun - Te Auaha, Wellington
https://www.teauahaevents.com/home/running-into-the-sun

After winning Best Theatre at the Melbourne Fringe, Running into the Sun comes home to Pōneke for the first time. The 10 creatives in a2 Company create an “exhausting but exhilarating” story of what it means to be alive right now with live music, dance and theatre.

The story follows Ash (Ben Ashby), Morris (Ethan Morse) and Mary (Jasmine Susic), at their university graduation house party. As they deal with personal heartbreaks, surprises and betrayals they must also reckon with a climate disaster unfolding in their hometown and a global future filled with war and political unrest. Dance, Clown, Live Music, Film and Physical Theatre weave together to catch the feeling of hope in the face of oncoming doom.

Running into the sun was developed by a2 Company; The Dance & Theatre production house co-directed by Nadiyah Akbar (Footnote NZ Dance, Joel Bray Dance) and Ben Ashby (Artistic Director of Long Cloud Youth Theatre 2020-2023).

“messy, joyous, heartfelt and completely off the chain.” - Melbourne Fringe Festival: Judges’ Picks.

“...suffocating and comforting …exhausting but exhilarating” - My Melbourne Arts

https://www.teauahaevents.com/home/running-into-the-sun

description:
Introduction to Lettterpress, This three and a half hour course is the perfect introduction to the traditional method of printing with lead type. Letterpress was the main method of printing for over 500 years from Johannes Gutenburg’s 1450s bible to recent times.

Letterpress has seen a huge revival in the United States and Britain while locally a number of artists and craftsmen have maintained the tradition. The three dimensional nature of letterpress is the perfect antidote to the flatness of digital print. This class will give you an insight into how the equipment, materials and techniques work.

The course will cover basic typography, composition, layout and letterpress techniques such as lockup, inking and paper selection on Adana tabletop platens and other proof presses as time allows.

Venue: WCBA. Wellington Centre for Book Arts, Woolstore Building, 262 Thorndon Quay, Wellington

Time: 9.00-12.30pm Sunday 12th October, 2025.

Cost: $85 (less 10% for members) includes 3.5 hours tuition, materials and letterpress booklet. Places are limited and can only be confirmed by payment.

Enrolments to: info@theprintingmuseum.org.nz
label: Classes & Workshops

description:
He Māori? Te Tiriti and Me Theatre Roadshow, Alongside the Wellington City Council Te Rā o Waitangi event, we welcome the free Theatre Roadshow 'He Māori?' by Īhaka Martyn, which is touring three locations on Waitangi Day:

10am – 11.30am: Tawa Community Centre
1.30pm – 3pm: Khandallah Community Centre
4pm – 5.30pm: The Hannah Playhouse

Performances are free, but bookings are required. Book your spot via the Council Te Rā o Waitangi event web page.

About He Māori?
Is 1/16th enough? Īhaka Martyn will have you laughing and crying as he weaves elements of stand-up comedy, waiata, and physical performance to explore the confusion, comedy and celebration of reclaiming his Māoritanga from a Pākehā upbringing. A 60 minute journey from total confusion to partial understanding. He aha? He Māori? Is he? Maybe - it depends.

He Māori? by Īhaka Martyn is presented in partnership with Taki Rua Productions, and is suitable for all ages. There will be a Q&A session after each performance.

description:
CMNZ Series Daniel Müller-Schott – Bach Solo Cello Suites, The six dances that comprise each of the six suites for solo cello provide the musical architecture around which Johann Sebastian Bach has explored the dichotomy of human emotion and intellect, creating some of the most extraordinary music ever composed for a solo instrument.
The cello suites are a touchstone work for cellists and carry with them a high risk-reward ratio. They are exposing, but in the hands of such a virtuoso as German cellist Daniel Müller-Schott, they are transcendent.

Described by the New York Times as “a fearless player with technique to burn”, Müller-Schott has been a soloist with leading international orchestras including the New York and Los Angeles Philharmonics, the Cleveland Orchestra, Boston, Chicago, Philadelphia, San Francisco and London Symphony Orchestras, the Berliner Philharmoniker, the Gewandhausorchester Leipzig, Bayrisches Staatsorchester and Münchner Philharmoniker.

Daniel Müller-Schott studied under Walter Nothas, Heinrich Schiff and Steven Isserlis. He was supported personally by Anne-Sophie Mutter and received, among other awards, the Aida Stucki Prize as well as a year of private tuition under Mstislaw Rostropovich. At the age of fifteen, Daniel Müller-Schott won the first prize at the International Tchaikovsky Competition for Young Musicians in 1992 in Moscow.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Jazz Festival: The Iggy Nama Ruci Quartet, An exclusive concert for Wellington Jazz Festival at The Undercurrent.

Led by Auckland saxophonist, Iggy Nama Ruci, the Iggy Nama Ruci quartet features a powerful interplay of musical conversation through the medium of straight-ahead jazz.

After recently touring around India and South Africa, surrounded by a plethora of Gospel jazz musicians, Iggy brings a fresh perspective and an open playfulness to the bandstand.

Michael Nash, fine-tuned Auckland pianist, draws inspiration from the likes of Brad Meldhau and Keith Jarrett, providing versatile and expressive approach to improvisation.

On double bass, Tyler Diprose acts as a grounding force, laying down a foundation, while providing rich musical intuition and connection in conversation with the band.

Max Crook brings a fiery finesse controlled by creativity and elegance to the drums. His attention to listening and reacting to the band demands constant interactive conversation.

The group will play a range of tunes from original compositions to standards by Thelonius Monk and Lennie Tristano.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Summer Soirée: Psychedelic & Melodic Techno feat Kozy (CA), Aztoria (, Wellington, join us for a night of Psychedelic & Melodic Techno featuring 2 international artists, Kozy ( Canada), Aztoria (Australia), supported by Aadhi, Blair & Uday. This event will be hosted at the legendary venue, Valhalla on the 24th of January, 8pm onwards.

Kozy will be playing a 2.5 Hour Extended Set, Aztoria will be playing a 1.5hr set & local legends will be playing an hour each.

International DJ Bio's:
KOZY ( CA)

KOZY aka Jeff Koz is a Montreal-based DJ & Entrepreneur specialising in dark & groovy underground techno music.

KOZY's dedication to music is evident in his tight mixes, solid track selection and inclination towards groovy synths and heavy, disruptive bass.

In 2019, he founded AlpaKa MuziK, a Montreal-based record label pushing world-class underground progressive, minimal and psychedelic techno beats. With releases from some of the biggest names in the game, AlpaKa MuziK has been paving the way for this fast emerging genre with relentless quality music and has become one of the most prominent labels in the psychedelic techno sub-genre.
With over 125 releases and over 650 tracks under their belt, AlpaKa has quickly become a powerhouse label in the underground techno community.

Since early 2022, in a little over 2 years, KOZY has gone on 9 international tours, including 5 euro tours, Australia, South Africa Mauritius, India, Mexico and has played over 150 gigs in 24 months in over 30 countries and counting.

Some prominent festivals he has performed at include, Esoteric in Australia, Hadra in France, Texas Eclipse, Boom Festival and many others, bringing to many of them the AlpaKa MuziK takeovers that showcase the cutting edge psychedelic techno sound through countless live sets around the world.
He recently finished his 4-month long 2025 summer euro tour where he performed at Boom Festival in Portugal and many more AlpaKa Showcases all around Europe and beyond. His 2025-2026 world tour is in motion, with Japan and a large Southern Hemisphere schedule in the works. With an unstoppable arsenal of unreleased, cutting edge bangers, he'll leave you wanting more after every set.

AZTORIA ( AU)

Aztoria is the psytech project of Ashley Zadel, also known as Flow Theory.
For more than 16 years, his melodic bush techno sound has evolved through auditory storytelling on stages across the globe, from Cape Town to London, Israel to Brazil, Portugal, New Zealand and Australia.


Rooted in progressive soundscapes with an organic flavour, Aztoria blends melodic deep house grooves with minimal techno to craft psychedelic atmospheres that ignite the imagination.

Aztoria burst onto the scene with his debut EP Reborn (Dense Nebula Records), hitting #4 on Beatport’s Techno charts. His 2024 single Birds of Paradise (Soliq Records) soared to #33, while his Inner Groove remix landed at #23 in Progressive House (Techgnosis Records). With fresh releases on Techgnosis, Recovery Collective and Puzzles Records and more in the works, Aztoria is carving his path in the underground with relentless energy and innovation.


Whether in the depths of a forest or the heart of a concrete jungle, Aztoria’s tunes will captivate and elevate you with his storytelling magic.
label: Music & Concerts

description:

Since humble beginnings in 2020, the project aims to create shared moments of joy with an aesthetic centred on obscure records. The success of this manifesto is reflected in the consistently positive response gained from partygoers and label supporters alike, year after year.⁣
⁣
In the DJ booth, Colter and Raffy (Craigslist Soundsystem) strike a delicate balance between eclecticism and danceability that denotes a deep understanding of musical traditions and club culture. Defying the confines of genre and era, the pair bring a refreshing and joyous sound to the dancefloor.⁣

Gordo & Teej are proud to present iconic Ōtautahi due Craigslist, after dancing to these guys all summer at Flux, Smash Palace, Electric Ave and Two Minds festivals we just had to fly 'em up to show the Pōneke whānau how these guys get the dance floor cranking. description:
Stage 1 Health and Safety Representatives, Stage 1 Health and Safety Representatives
This course introduces you to the key elements of effective worker participation and engagement.

About this Event

The purpose of this two-day course is to prepare anyone who wants to be officially recognised as an H&S Representative in their workplace. The course introduces you to the key elements of effective worker participation and engagement. It also covers the key strategies and tools you will need to be an effective representative in your workplace.


Key audience:
This course is suitable for people who have been chosen to be the H&S Representative for their organisation and need to be well-prepared for the role. It would also be useful for anyone who would like to know about the requirements for the role or who have employees in these positions.


Learning outcomes
Understand the key duty holders and their respective duties under the Health & Safety at Work Act 2015.
Learn how to identify and report on links to low productivity due to health and safety practices.
Generate improvements to reduce injury costs, occurrence and rehabilitation.

If you would like to register with Business Central directly and receive an invoice for payment, please contact our training team via .


Course details
Duration: two full days, in person.
Time: 9am-4pm
NZQA Unit Standard: 29315 (Assessment costs additional)

description:
Banks Arcade // Wellington // NU - Zealand Tour // With Ocean Grove & No Life, BANKS ARCADE ANNOUNCE TRIUMPHANT RETURN TO NEW ZEALAND FOR TWO EXPLOSIVE SHOWS
Auckland, New Zealand — 19th December 2025 — After a monumental few years sharing stages with global heavyweights including Highly Suspect, Northlane, and The Plot In You, and selling out headline shows across Australia and New Zealand, Banks Arcade have announced their highly anticipated return home this December. The genre-bending powerhouse will headline two unmissable nights on December 19 and December 20, marking a powerful homecoming to New Zealand stages.
Fans can expect an electrifying set packed with the band's fierce catalogue of anthems, showcasing the explosive live energy that has cemented them as one of the fastest-rising forces in heavy music.
Joining the lineup are Melbourne's genre-defying trailblazers Ocean Grove, whose wild live shows have earned them a reputation as one of Australia's most unpredictable acts. Also on board are No Life, New Zealand's rising heavy hitters, ready to set the tone with their unrelenting intensity.
Quote from the band
"Coming back to New Zealand to play these shows feels like a full-circle moment for us. This is where it all began, and we can't wait to bring the energy home and share the stage with some of our closest friends," says Banks Arcade.
Tickets are on sale now at www.banksarcade.com — get in quick, these will move fast.

description:
The Check-in Men's Group Tuesday 30 Sept, To join this community, and book your Tuesdays - please sign up here first, and we will email you the event access code. (our venue address will be emailed with your ticket).

This is a space for men to talk honestly, listen deeply, and figure some things out together. It’s not about having it all sorted. You don’t need to be a certain type of guy. Just show up as you are, with no pressure to speak or share.
Open group: new faces are welcome
Flexible: book to come when it suits you. Each Tuesday is a new event. Most will try for fortnightly.
Small groups: 8 men + 2 experienced hosts
Inclusive: all ages, backgrounds and identities (18+)
It's about connection and showing up, instead of going it alone. It makes time for meeting our various challenges, while learning and growing us into better friends, partners, dads, sons and humans. 
It isn’t a boys or bros club, therapy or a self-help seminar, religious or new-age. No fixing, preaching, or pressure. It’s not a crisis service or a substitute for professional care. The sign-up form explains this in more detail.
You can expect to:
Practice listening and learn communication skills for life
Discover greater integrity and self-accountability
Be supported in building your emotional intelligence
Become a part of a hearty and diverse community
Have a good time - it’s often uplifting and inspiring.
Joseph and your team of hosts have all been in regular men's groups for many years. The group sometimes offers simple proven inquiries that draw on NVC, MBSR, active listening, gratitude and more.
label: Health & Wellness

description:
Seedlings – Group Show, Seedlings brings together eighteen artists who have each contributed to the ongoing journey of Twentysix Gallery. Taking its title from the season of spring, this group show reflects the quiet energy of growth — each artist sharing a single work that speaks to where they are now and the evolving life of the gallery community.
The show spans painting, photography, textiles, ceramics and sculpture, reflecting the range of practices nurtured here over the years. What unites these works is not a shared theme but a shared spirit of generosity, friendship, and dialogue. Together they speak to the quiet ways ideas take root, shift, and flourish — nurtured by artists who have been with us since the gallery’s beginnings as well as those newly joining the fold.
Featuring Aida Smith, Chloe Mason, Chora Carleton, David Brown, Emma Hercus, Gina Kiel, Jess Swney, Lewis Cosgrave, Lindsey Horne, Nan Mulder, Pam Brabants, Petra Scheuber, Russell Kleyn, Simon Mark, Suzanne Lustig, Thomas Strawbridge, Will Bennett, Yoan Jolly.
We’re celebrating the opening on Friday, 24th October at 6pm with a community BBQ and live music from the Rongotai College Big Band. BYO drinks and something to throw on the grill. Kindly sponsored by Culley’s and Behemoth Brewing.
The exhibition runs from 25 October – 15 November 2025 at Twentysix Gallery.
Opening Hours:

description:
Capital Blues Presents: BB and the Bullets, “An authentic, powerful and generous album, which will undoubtedly place BB & The Bullets among the blues rock revelations of the year.” – Zicazic Magazine, France
It’s been a great couple of weeks for dynamic Whanganui blues band BB & THE BULLETS! 
Currently playing their North Island tour, celebrating the international release of their debut album HIGH TIDE from September, the album has achieved impressive traction across Aotearoa’s music soundscape by entering the Official Top 20 Aotearoa Albums at #12 and #5 on the Independent Music NZ Charts!
Landing on both the IMNZ Charts and the Official Aotearoa Music Charts is a cracking result, not just as independent artists, but also for a blues-rock outfit, demonstrating that it’s a genre that can successfully cross both indie and conventional charts, appealing to both grassroots and mainstream audiences – an outcome that the band is thrilled about.
“It’s a great result! We are so proud to be #5 on the IMNZ Chart. Independent artists are the grassroots and lifeblood of Kiwi music. And there’s only been a few local indie blues-rock albums on the Official Aotearoa Music Charts over the last decade – our mate Grant Haua in 2022, and Troy Kingi in 2016, so we’re stoked to be here and rep the Blues in 2025!”
The tight, three piece blues rock outfit of band members Brad McMillan (drums), Stu Duncan (bass), and Brian Baker (guitar / vocals)  have garnered a reputation for their powerful and moving live performances, drawing comparisons to Stevie Ray Vaughan, Johnny Winter, Peter Green and Jeff Beck, while firmly putting their own stamp on the timeless medium that is the blues.
BB & The Bullets have released three well received singles to date off the album: debut single ‘Something In The Water’; album title track ‘High Tide’; and their blues-soaked rendition of the Beatles classic ‘I Want You (She’s So Heavy)’ which is a huge fan favourite at their live shows. To date, all three singles have enjoyed some excellent blues radio attention, including airplay on Radio New Zealand and around the country, as well as in Germany, USA, and Canada, with ‘High Tide’ charting at #4 on Roots Music Charts in Australia, achieving #1 on the Roots Music Report Blues Album Chart, #1 on the Blues Rock Album Chart and three songs on the Blues Singles chart.
Home country of their label Dixiefrog Records has plenty of great things to say about BB & The Bullets, with Paris Move Magazine giving the album a very favourable review, ranking it as a ‘red heart’ favourite, as has French outlet Zicazic Magazine, and the title track single ‘High Tide’ has made the weekly playlist of Rolling Stone Magazine, France.
“This album proves to be a declaration of love to the roots of the blues, driven by striking live energy and unabashed virtuosity.” – Zicazic Magazine, France
With a well-deserved reputation for their compelling live shows, BB & The Bullets audiences are always wowed by the power and intensity of their performances.
With this chart action, it is evident that the blues scene is alive and vibrant on Antipodean shores, grab your mates and don’t miss your chance to check out a blues-rock experience like no other with BB & The Bullets!
label: Music & Concerts

FLINT Wellington: Get to know a CxO, Ever wondered what it really takes to make it to the top? Join us for an evening of speed networking with some of Wellington’s most inspiring executives.

This isn’t your typical panel event. It’s relaxed, interactive, and designed to help you connect directly with the people behind the titles. Whether you’re curious about leadership, thinking about your next move, or just love hearing great career stories, this event’s for you.
Come for the insights, stay for the connections ✨

🎙️ Speakers: 
Anna Darling, GM People Experience at Sharesies
Phillip Whitmore, Partner - Digital at KPMG
Anna Mitchell, Executive General Manager, Frontier at Chorus
More to be announced!
Spots are limited, with FLINT members taking priority over non-members so register now to secure your spot!
We look forward to seeing you there.
_______________________
About FLINT
If you’re in the early stages of your career and you’re interested in digital technologies, come join the FLINT programme in your local city. We host events, webinars, podcasts and social catch ups that offer valuable networking, learning and growth opportunities. 
Find out more about the Wellington FLINT team here.
  Anna Mitchell, Executive General Manager, Frontier at Chorus
Anna is Executive General Manager, Frontier at Chorus, with accountabilities for fibre footprint growth, copper exit, property optimisation, and corporate affairs, as well as championing Chorus’ sustainability and digital equity ambitions. She brings broad expertise in telecommunications policy and leading strategic initiatives, with experience across both public and private sectors, including roles at Deloitte, SweeneyVesty, and as a senior advisor to a cabinet minister.
  Philip Whitmore, Partner, Cyber and Digital at KPMG
Philip is a Partner within KPMG Cyber with over 30 years’ experience across a broad range of cyber security disciplines. He has worked extensively in New Zealand, as well as throughout the wider Asia Pacific region.  His cyber security knowledge and experience is supported by his wider business consulting background, giving him the ability to readily translate cyber security risks into a business context. Philip’s experience saw him win the New Zealand Cyber Security Person of the Year award in 2023.
  Anna Darling, GM People Experience at Sharesies
Anna is an experienced HR leader with 15 years’ experience across the public and private sectors in New Zealand and the UK. Passionate about human-centred design, she focuses on creating meaningful employee experiences and driving diversity, equity, and inclusion. Anna is especially committed to increasing Māori and Pasifika representation in tech and expanding opportunities for underrepresented groups, drawing on her background in developing women’s rugby and promoting gender equity in sport in Aotearoa. She also serves on the Board of Nuku Ora and was part of the Sharesies team recognised as the Overall 2025 Best Place to Work in October.

description:
How to Build a Gate, 
Liza has always been the smartest person in the room, especially when she's alone... at least she used to be.
A chronic overachiever and textbook narcissist with a caffeine addiction that would rival most Starbucks card holders, she's built her entire identity around being exceptional. So when she's chosen to spearhead a cutting-edge AI project, it feels less like an opportunity and more like a gift-- no, a prayer from God.
But exceptional though she may be, she's still human, and still has those human needs we hate to ignore. So when a hot neighbour comes knocking, she just can't run from temptation. Too bad that neighbor might be a little closer to home than she thinks?
Extra cupa sugar, anyone?
As her creation begins to outsmart, out-feel, and out-moral its maker, Liza finds herself asking the one question she never thought she would: what happens when being "the best" means you've made something better?
Fresh off the finale of Soho Playhouse Lighthouse series in New York, How to Build a Gate is back in New Zealand bigger and better than ever!
Featuring Kate Low and American actor Megan Piggott, How to Build a Gate is a razor-sharp new comedy about control, competition, and the illusion of perfection. In a world that worships innovation though it has long forgotten why, the play takes ambition, ethics, and the burnout of trying to matter under a microscopic glass.
From playwright Electra Artemis--whose self proclaimed "hooker with a heart of gold" writing style will keep you laughing right til it doesn't--this Fringe premiere reimagines the God vs Man myth for the startup age. Equal parts satire, soul-searching and a heroine with rockin' a-cups, How to Build a Gate invites audiences to laugh, cringe, and maybe see themselves in Liza's heartfelt, heartless mania.

description:
Tap for Uprooting the Past — Let Go & Move Forward, Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars
Workshop Series: Uprooting the Past — Let Go & Move Forward
Sometimes the past doesn't stay in the past. It lives in our thoughts, our reactions, our choices—quietly shaping the way we move through life. But what if we could uproot those old patterns, make peace with what was, and step forward with clarity and purpose?
This transformative series brings together four unique workshops, each offering a different lens on the same powerful journey: letting go, healing, and moving forward.
Across four sessions with different facilitators, you'll explore diverse approaches to:
Uprooting the past — Releasing what no longer serves you
Making peace — Finding closure and compassion for yourself
Gaining new perspectives — Seeing your story through fresh eyes
Learning and growing — Integrating wisdom that moves you forward
Creating a powerful pathway toward freedom, peace, and new beginnings.
Meet your host, Krishnaloka
Introducing Krishnaloka, who is driven to help others overcome their issues within their mind and pyschology. As a mind technician trained in eutaptics (Faster EFT), she employs the fastest and most powerful method to create lasting change by addressing the root causes of issues rather than just the symptoms. Her warm and caring nature makes every interaction with her both comforting and transformative.
6.15pm Tuesday 4th of November
End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.

description:
Ludovico Einaudi, His music has been performed live on an Arctic ice-floe and as soundtracks for Oscar and Golden Globe-winning films. Italian pianist and composer Ludovico Einaudi is returning to Wellington with a new album to explore with audiences. The Michael Fowler Centre will be filled with Ludovico’s special mix of neo-classical, pop, ambient, rock, and experimental styles. His modern soundscapes are renowned for being able to cross artistic and generational boundaries. Because of this, he has become one of the world’s most-streamed classical music artists. 
Ludovico began composing in the 1980s for theatre and ballet. He has since gone on to write for film and television and produce multi-million-selling albums. His most recent is ‘The Summer Portraits’. Written in 2025, it is the result of a cascade of personal memories triggered by sun, summer, and family holidays. Here, he returns to the lush full-band textures of earlier works. You’ll get to hear this first-hand, so don’t miss your chance.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Jan Boogie Woogie Preston Duo Plays The Shed, JAN PRESTON is a knockout singer and pianist who has captured audiences around the world.
Delighted to be returning to Wellington in March, Jan has a special connection with the area, where she lived before moving to Sydney. Indeed in Wellington she played in her first bandwith NZ blues icon, Midge Marsden, friend and mentor.
She has now created her own blues/boogie style trademarked by exceptional piano playing together with her vibrant singing and songwriting.
Jan also composes soundtracks, including music for her sister Dame Gaylene Preston’s films.
Together with her drummer Mike Pullman, Jan’s boogie groove is magnetic. Prepare to move  and tap your feet! 
The  two set show will highlight the recent 50 Years In Pianoland album .
Where the  irrepressible Jan Preston comes clean on her longstanding and sometimes bumpy road to piano stardom. Jan will spin yarns from dives in Manhattan to the concert halls of Europe, from her early days in Wellington’s Red Mole Theatre Troupe to a pop hit with Auckland rock band Coup D’Etat, relocating to Sydney, and her enduring 50 year career as a piano player, singer/songwriter and composer. Jan’s duo will perform her original songs and compositions, providing a perfect counterpoint to her story.
The Shed is an unlicensed seated venue.
Doors open 3PM for a 3.30 Show.
 One woman, a piano and a whole lot of talent.
3 Weeks Edinburgh
 From boogie woogie to ragtime the air pulsates with the magical rhythms she produces….a forceful presence and a huge performance energy.
ChCh Star NZ
 One of the best pianists of our time, Preston masters the piano like a maestro juggling the keys. Her voice is suave and sensual, with finesse and sensibility.
Crossroads France
 Australia’s finest female blues talent
Drum Media Sydney
 This New Zealander is a discovery for friends of excellent boogie based music.
Blues News Germany
 Jan Preston is a crusader of the keys…she carries the torch for the piano culture.
Melbourne Age
 She sings with a delivery which speaks of empowerment and hope….she’s got the blues in her heart, mardi gras in her fingers, and a Trans-Tasman attitude in her songs.
Rhythms Magazine Australia
 At the piano she is queen.
ChCh Press NZ
 Technically strong, full of character, dripping with life.
American Blues Review

description:
Impactful Leadership, Impactful Leadership:
What do horses, mules and the All Blacks have in common? Hear a true expert break-down what really works when it comes to impactful leadership.
Welcome to the final conversation in our Women in Business series: four events that bring you important conversations led by Women in Business and heard by everyone. In partnership with Forsyth Barr, this bar-setting series is bringing hard-hitting topics into the conversational spotlight.
What good leadership looks like has changed, but many of the methods we rely on to deliver it haven’t. This conversation will showcase what horses can teach us about impactful leadership in modern workplaces, and how anyone can turn those lessons into action that delivers.
Why be part of this conversation?
In this thought-provoking session, BBC journalist-turned-leadership coach Janine Sudbury will share how she discovered what really works in leadership development, and why traditional approaches often fall short.
You’ll take away insights including:
The three secrets of the horse whisperer that unlock authentic, presence-based leadership
How to overcome Imposter Syndrome and People Pleasing to lead with clarity and confidence
Why horses give the most honest feedback—and how that feedback creates lasting breakthroughs
Who’s the speaker?
Janine Sudbury is the founder of Impact Leadership and the creator of a truly unique experiential leadership programme that swaps the boardroom for the paddock. specialises in helping women overcome Imposter Syndrome and People Pleasing Syndrome to lead with more confidence, clarity, and authenticity.
With powerful insights and unforgettable stories, including her work with former All Blacks coach Steve Hansen, Janine invites you to lead with more purpose, presence, and impact.
What’s in-store:
5:15-5:30pm – Arrival/networking
5:30-6:15pm – Janine Sudbury speaks
6:15-6:30pm – Q&A
6:30-7:30pm – Networking with drinks & canapes
Business card prize draw:
Attend the event and go in the draw to win a “New BMW for a Weekend” and a “New MINI for a Weekend” from Continental Cars BMW and Wellington MINI Garage.
This event is part of the Women in Business chapter from our “Join the Conversation” series - 4 events that spotlight important topics through conversation, led by the amazing women driving change within them.
Attend other events in the series:
17 July: Making money in the mess - How every woman can financially thrive in messy economic times
7 August: Strengths & Superpowers - Personal branding isn’t showing off - it’s taking ownership over how you’re perceived. Join a Masterclass in making yours standout among the rest
20 August: 3 Gals, One Beehive – live! - Hear three leading women take a week in politics, and turn it into the unfiltered commentary that you wish you’d hear on the 6 o’clock news.

description:
Sexy Ghost Boy, 
Begin with a circle. Work your way up, slowly. Bravely. The Macarena happens to completion. Scream.
This is how you summon Sexy Ghost Boy, the award winning and hilariously f***able spirit haunting Whiskey & Wood this Fringe.
The show dissects the unspoken rituals and micro neurosis we have around sex and the presentation of ourselves as sexual agents. Satirical in its burlesque and invigorating in its clown, this wordless performance inspires the audience to heroically rebel against insecurity.
Described by witnesses as a hot mess mash up of Mr Bean and Marina Abramović, it has split the sides of audiences across New Zealand and is boldly propositioning Wellingtonians to have an unforgettable evening of Theatre.
"A theatrical alchemist, Fenn has the rare talent to create something ridiculous yet very clever." - Rosheen Fitzgerald, Theatreview

"I left with a sore throat and vaguely manic euphoria from laughing so uproariously" Ellen Murray, Theatreview

"we both feel so... I don't even know how to describe it. I feel like I'm floating while simultaneously feeling grounded, I'm ecstatic while also very chill. Maybe it's the full moon, or maybe I'm just a little bit possessed by the Sexy Ghost Boy" Laura Ferguson, Art Murmurs
Sexy Ghost Boy is also on at Sweet Axe Throwing Co. from the 25th - 28th of February.
label: Arts & Theatre

description:
CRYPTOPSY - ALL SO VILE - NZ 2025 - WELLINGTON, CRYPTOPSY - ALL SO VILE - NZ 2025 - WELLINGTON
+++++ 𝐂𝐑𝐘𝐏𝐓𝐎𝐏𝐒𝐘 - 𝐀𝐋𝐋 𝐒𝐎 𝐕𝐈𝐋𝐄 - 𝐍𝐙 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓 𝐓𝐎𝐔𝐑 +++++

A vital force in Canadian technical death metal from 1993 onwards, 𝗖𝗿𝘆𝗽𝘁𝗼𝗽𝘀𝘆 won the hearts of fans worldwide with the masterful viciousness of their mid-'90s double-punch classics Blasphemy Made Flesh (1994) and None so Vile (1996). Featuring founding member / drumming maestro Flo Mounier alongside gravel-toned vocalist Matt McGachy, guitarist Christian Donaldson (both of whom have been with the group for two decades) and bassist since 2012 Olivier Pinard, 𝗖𝗿𝘆𝗽𝘁𝗼𝗽𝘀𝘆's Grammy award-winning album As Gomorrah Burns (2023, Nuclear Blast) signalled a productive new era for the unrepentantly aggressive Montreal four-piece, who have vowed to release a new record every two years.

𝗖𝗿𝘆𝗽𝘁𝗼𝗽𝘀𝘆 are bringing their 𝗔𝗟𝗟 𝗦𝗢 𝗩𝗜𝗟𝗘 tour down under this December just before Christmas, blasting local punters with sounds from their ninth studio album An Insatiable Violence and more!


𝐂𝐑𝐘𝐏𝐓𝐎𝐏𝐒𝐘 - 𝐀𝐋𝐋 𝐒𝐎 𝐕𝐈𝐋𝐄 - 𝐍𝐙 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓 𝐓𝐎𝐔𝐑

description:
Photography Symposium – Slow Burn, To celebrate the upcoming photography exhibition Slow Burn | Ahi Tāmau, opening in 2026, we're hosting a day of collegial conversation about contemporary photography in Aotearoa. Slow Burn highlights the contribution of women and non-binary photographers, drawing on over a decade of research and collecting.

Tickets $60 (waged) / $40 (unwaged) with lunch included.

Held on opening day, this symposium begins with a special opportunity to view the exhibition before the museum opens to the public, followed by a keynote presentation from Natalie Robertson, a leading photographer, moving-image artist, and Associate Professor at Auckland University of Technology. Her practice centres her ancestral river, Waiapu, exploring how “lens-based practice can contribute to iwi-based aspirations for environmental reinvigoration”.

Across a series of discussions with Aotearoa photographers, we will explore key threads in contemporary practice – including collaboration, process, and evolving photographic culture – offering insights into the ideas and relationships that shape photography today. We'll close the day in the galleries, with a new performance work by Ann Shelton from the physical garden series.

Open to photographers, curators, researchers, students, and anyone with an interest in photography, this event invites participants to connect, reflect, and share.

Deacon Blue will be stopping at the James Hay Theatre to play in Ōtautahi, Christchurch for the first time ever on Thursday February 12, before heading to Pōneke, Wellington’s Opera House on Saturday February 14, followed by Sunday February 15 at the Bruce Mason Centre in Tāmaki Makaurau, Auckland.

New Zealand fans can expect a specially curated setlist, including all the hits you know and love, blending arena-sized classic anthems, tracks off their latest album, mixed with intimate surprises. With a reputation for crafting unforgettable live moments, Deacon Blue’s 2026 tour promises to be a celebration of fan connection, nostalgia, and musical brilliance.

description:
PING PONG BALLER, 
Winning in life isn’t about the size of your balls.
It’s about how you spin them.
Wouter Moerloose presents his debut stand-up hour about the triumph of attitude over accolades.
This Belgian raconteur spins sharp, self-aware stories from his chaotic journey towards the only thing he never knew he wanted: to make you laugh!
Expect crying on planes, poorly chosen hobbies, and an unhealthy obsession with celebrities’ wealth – featuring (without their consent) an AK-47 hoarding grandfather, Latvia’s worst kidnapper, and some surprisingly horny sea mammals.
PING PONG BALLER: the nearly-award winning show where almost does count!
Wouter’s accolades (just in case):

description:
It's in the Ballot - CDC – Carterton Councilor at Large -In-studio, Contestants of 'It's in the Bag' become political candidates in 'It's in the Ballot' Meet the Candidates.
Candidates choose a numbered box which has a question that has been submitted online by voters before the show.
It’s in the Ballot” is a community-led awareness and participation initiative. We aim to increase voter interest in the general and council elections nationally.
By using broadcasting and web-based technologies to deliver in-studio; online on Facebook, YouTube and Twitch; and edited as YouTube videos we aim to enable voters to make informed voting decisions.
This means you can send the weblink of the YouTube Video of your electorate show to your friends and whanau to encourage them to watch the show before they cast their votes.
Voting papers are sent to voters on Tuesday 9th September 2025 and must be delivered into a voting dropbox by 12pm Saturday 11th October 2025

description:
Finding Love, After All @ NZ Fringe, ‘He’s nursed a broken heart, so he learned his lessons’

An evening of music and poetry reflecting on what self-love actually looks like, through the lens of dating and relationships. Cute moments (like childhood crushes) open the door to deeper conversations about finding a peaceful, accepting and understanding relationship with yourself.

So basically, it’s like if you imagine advice on dating and how to navigate the complicated intricacies of love, but you find out that the intricacies you’re navigating are with yourself, your history, your wounds, and learning to accept your own flaws like you would with someone you are learning to love.

------------------------------------

I’m joined on stage by two wildly talented friends. Ellie, a jazz trained singer for gorgeous duets and a Hannah on violin to make love songs soar. Hosted at The Undercurrent, the beautiful second-hand bookstore, expect a warm, cozy evening of heart-forward art.

Awards for previous show 'For My Family, And Yours'
- Most Promising Pacific Artist – Dunedin Fringe 2025
- Collective Catharsis Award – Hastings Fringe 2024

And a massive thank you to NZ Fringe and The FATU Grant for your funding. Thank you for the support to make the show I dreamed of

description:
Journey into Om, Journey into Om
Yoga without mantra is a bit like cake without the icing!

By now, we all know that everything essentially is vibration.
Yoga knew this 1000's of years ago, that is why sound is the first tool of Yoga.
The use of a mantra is a powerful tool for healing, emotional and mental clarity, and spiritual development.
Learn about the pranavah mantra ( om) as it relates to Yoga Practice.
In this 2-hour workshop, you will be taught the traditional and respectful way to use this sacred sound and what it means.

We will draw on the ancient texts of Yoga as our point of reference (Patanjali's Yoga Sutras).

You will be guided in a variety of accessible practices to experience the effects of this mantra.
This workshop can open new dimensions to your practice, or it is also a great way to start on the Yoga Journey.

I teach from the Viniyoga tradition of Krishnamacharya, where mantra is used as an integral aspect of Yoga, whether it be asana, pranayama, or meditation.
This workshop will be nourishing to all the dimensions -heart, mind, and body.
You do not need to be a singer at all-just be willing to have a go at making a sound, even in your head if out loud feels too weird.Tickets $40 from Humanatrix https://events.humanitix.com/journey-into-om/tickets

description:
A Man Named Dave, 
A Man Named Dave tells the story of an ordinary man with an extraordinary problem. Dave lives a perfectly normal life--so normal, in fact, that every moment is dictated by The Narrator. Day after day, he trudges through his monotonous 9-5, until one morning he decides to flip the script.
What follows is a chaotic and playful journey to independence, as Dave wrestles for control of his own story. Along the way, his co-workers--appliances, office supplies, and unexpected allies--spring to life. Some help him, some hinder him, but all push him closer to discovering what it really means to take charge of his own destiny.
Blending voiceover, puppetry, and live performance, A Man Named Dave is a witty and imaginative exploration of self-determination in an absurd world.

description:
Hanging decorations clay workshop, Hanging decorations clay workshop
Create gorgeous colourful clay hanging decorations using my unique hand drawn templates and a range of creative tools and techniques, adding colour with underglazes and slips.

Around 5 weeks after the class these will be fired and glazed using a clear glaze to bring out the striking colours, creating gorgeous art pieces to hang in your home or gift to another.

Make up to 3 large (approx 13cm) or 4 small (approx 7cm) or a combination.

Working with clay is good for the soul! Immersing in a gentle creative activity helps to switch off a busy mind, and making tiles is fun and achievable even if this is your first time using clay.

All materials are provided so all you need to do is turn up ready to have some fun and creativity.

Booking is essential so please use the ticket link provided or contact

$95 per person, includes all materials, glaze and double kiln firing.
Completed pieces will be available to collect from Normandale or a subsequent class in Pauahatanui.

For those unable to collect, I offer the convenience of postal delivery for an additional fee.

description:
Mexican Prehispanic Experience at AWHI, Join Claudio in his Mexican project called Xochipilli´s Eye. Xochipilli (Pronounce: Zo-chee-pee-lee) is the Mexica god of music, flowers, arts, and sacred plants.
Claudio´s last trip home to Mexico was full of inspiration, reconnecting him to his ancestral roots in Mexico City. He was fortunate to meet a prehispanic musician and instrument maker who plays and crafts beautiful traditional instruments. Claudio returned to NZ with many special new instruments; and continues to be inspired and guided by the cultural wealth, wisdom, and knowledge of the ancient cultures of the Americas.
This event will be extraordinary and unique. You will learn about Mexican prehispanic cosmology and spirituality. Experience simple and powerful rituals together with unique musical instruments used by Olmecs, Toltecs, Aztecs, and Mayans. The instruments include clay flutes, clay drums, medicine drums, ocarinas, wooden flutes, shells, and shakers skilfully arranged to create magical shamanic atmospheres.
We will be sharing Cacao which is an energetic traditional Mexican prehispanic drinks that help open the heart, reconnect with the earth element, ignite your fire, and get you up and moving.
What to expect:
Cleansing copal and herbal welcoming.
Talk about the use of music in the ancient times.
An opening ceremony to acknowledge the Tlalmanalli (offering and altar) with the deep sound of the conch shell in the four directions.
Cacao ceremony: Claudio will offer the cacao drink as a connection with the earth, to yourself, and the moment.
Live music that invokes many emotions and imagery, from contemplative natural and mystical environments to uplifting and empowering rhythms. You are free to project and explore within and/ or express and liberate projecting outwards.
We will finish with a shamanic atmosphere relaxation and close the event with a traditional chant.
No previous experience is required.
What to bring:
Wear comfortable clothing! Bring an open heart and a mind ready to learn and receive. You are also welcome to bring a fruit and or a flower to contribute to the altar.
Date & Time: Saturday, April 13th, 6-7:30pm
Location: AWHI Yoga & Wellbeing, Level 2, 15 Johnston Street, Wellington Central
Investment: $55

ELVIS AN AMERICAN TRILOGY
Presents
ALOHA FROM HAWAII”

Starring 3 best Elvis tribute artists in the world: Steve Michaels, Louis Brown and Arron Walker

Due to overwhelming demand, Elvis: An American Trilogy, the show that embodies the essence of ‘The King’, is coming to NZ. The show that takes the audience on a spellbinding journey through the many eras of the legendary Elvis Presley.

The show begins with Louis Brown who captures the audience with his raw rockabilly sound and signature dance moves.

Arron Walker takes the role of Elvis in his movie years and the ‘68 Comeback Special as he effortlessly recreates the straight-out-of-the-army look and into the black leather, personifying this period with all the songs and sex appeal that was Elvis.

Steve Michaels is also arguably the greatest exponent of Elvis ‘Aloha from Hawaii’-era in the world, the look, the sound, and the moves, combined with the shy and charming Elvis’s disposition will make ‘Elvis and American Trilogy’ THE Elvis show to see in 2025

If you only see one Elvis show in 2025 make it …Elvis: An American Trilogy

description:
Cultivating a Positive Workplace Culture for High-Performance Teams, Cultivating a Positive Workplace Culture for High-Performance Teams
This course is designed for leaders, managers, and organisational change agents who want to foster an impactful, positive workplace culture.

About this Event

Participants will explore the essential principles of creating an environment that nurtures trust, accountability, inclusivity, and engagement. Through practical strategies, case studies, and interactive discussions, participants will learn how to develop a culture that motivates teams to excel, encourages open communication, and supports professional growth.


Key Audience:
Leaders, managers, and organisational change agents who want to foster a positive workplace culture that drives high performance, collaboration, and employee wellbeing.


Key Topics Include:
• Building a culture of trust and accountability
• Effective communication and feedback loops that positively impact overall performance
• Aligning values and purpose to improve collaboration
• Understanding the true power of inclusivity, diversity, and belonging
• Promoting autonomy and valuing innovation
• Encouraging wellbeing and care
• Developing strategies for conflict resolution
• Strategies and tools to increase positive engagement

By the end of the course, participants will have the tools and insights needed to create a workplace environment that not only supports but accelerates the success of high-performing teams, driving both individual and collective achievement.


Learning Outcomes
By the end of this course, participants will be able to:
Implement strategies to cultivate trust, psychological safety, and high performance within their teams.
Lead teams that foster open communication, collaboration, and inclusivity.
Align team goals with organisational values and objectives, ensuring a shared sense of purpose.
Develop effective strategies for conflict resolution and team cohesion.
Create a workplace culture that encourages growth, well-being, and work-life balance.

description:
Gong Bath at Newtown Community Centre, ***$25***
Limited door sales for $30
*Please bring a yoga mat, pillow, eye mask and blanket for maximum comfort and warmth*
Discover the transformative effects of sound therapy with this 1-hour gong bath experience.
During this session, participants will be surrounded by the resonant vibrations of gongs, carefully designed to induce deep states of relaxation and calm. As you settle into the experience, allow the soothing tones to wash over you, releasing tension and promoting a sense of inner peace.
Scientific studies have highlighted the benefits of sound therapy, particularly gong baths, in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The rhythmic patterns and frequencies of the gongs can help entrain brainwaves, leading to a meditative state and improved mental clarity.
Beyond the immediate effects, many participants report experiencing lasting benefits such as improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and enhanced mood following a gong bath session. By immersing yourself in the healing vibrations of the gongs, you can tap into a deeper sense of relaxation and rejuvenation.
Whether you're looking to unwind after a long day or explore new avenues of self-care, this 1-hour gong bath offers a unique opportunity to reconnect with yourself and find balance amidst the hustle and bustle of daily life. There will be cups of tea available afterwards to help us integrate and ground
**THIS EVENT IS RESTRICTED TO 20 PEOPLE ONLY**
Disclaimer
Gong baths are safe and supportive for most people. However, please consult your doctor or mental health professional before attending if you:
-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)
If you have recent injuries, surgeries, or other health concerns, let me know so we can make sure you’re comfortable.
Everyone responds differently to sound. If at any point during the session you feel overwhelmed or uncomfortable, you are welcome to sit up, move gently, or step outside. Your wellbeing always comes first.
About Me
Erika is a musician and astrologer based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. She has 30+ years musical experience. Erika has an interest in the healing properties of sound for mental and physical wellbeing, which she currently explores through her gong bathing practice

description:
Portrait Play: Natural Light Photography Workshop with Sony Scene, Practice and learn how to pose and photograph people
Learn, practice and play with portrait photography!
Curious about portraits but not sure where to start? This welcoming workshop is designed especially for beginners who want to build confidence behind the camera in a relaxed, supportive setting.
With Wellington portrait photographer Rebecca McMillan as your guide, you’ll explore how to capture people using just the light around you. No flash or fancy lighting required. Our slightly spooky late-October theme adds a fun twist, and with a professional model to work with, you’ll get plenty of chances to practice both taking photos and gently directing your subject.
Over two and a half hours, Rebecca will share how she approaches a scene, engages with her subject, and makes the most of natural light in an outdoor city location. You'll then have plenty of time to practice photography and directing our model. You’ll leave with new skills, a boost of confidence, and portraits you’ll be proud of.
The workshop is an outdoor session in a central Wellington location. Location details will be communicated to registered attendees.
Register at Sony Scene: all camera brands welcome!

description:
The Music of Greece, Marking its Aotearoa debut, the Hellenic Music Ensemble brings the unforgettable soundscape of Greece to life in a landmark global tour. The group will be under the direction of acclaimed composer and conductor Panos Liaropoulos. This 21-piece orchestra honours the genius of Greece’s most iconic composers: Mikis Theodorakis (BAFTA-winning ‘Zorba's Dance’) and Manos Hadjidakis (‘Never on a Sunday’). 
The orchestra will be joined by legendary voices Manolis Mitsias, Giannis Haroulis, and Natassa Bofiliou. Together, they’ll bring the quintessential melodies of Greece to the Michael Fowler Centre. This one-night-only show is part of the Aotearoa New Zealand Festival of the Arts. Soar through a century of song — celebrating the soul, poetry, and passion of Greece.

description:
Capital Blues Presents: Simon Donald, The recent Ash Grunwald show at meowNui was one of those rare shows where the support act is spoken of in a good way, in a VERY good way.
Lots of people were saying what a great show Simon Donald gave. Gritty blues, played with passion and skill. And played by a local musician too. What more can you ask for? So we booked him.
Simon Donald—understated kiwi brilliance. Chosen to support Ash Grunwald, and now taking the stage at Capital Blues. His online presence is about everyone else. This time, we’re making it all about him.
If you’ve caught him live at meowNUI, Paekākāriki, or the Kapiti Boating Club, you already know: Simon plays from the gut. Blues, Americana, and that rare kind of tone that feels lived-in and honest. He’s shared stages with Ash Grunwald, Sig Wilder, and National Sensation, and the feedback speaks for itself. As Ash put it:
“Hey brother you were amazing. Great to hear some good blues
”
Simon’s sets are all about feel. Deep blues, warm swing, and the kind of playing that makes you lean in and listen. Whether you’re a Thursday regular or just blues-curious, this one’s worth showing up for.
Members get discounted entry and 10% off at the bar—so come on down, grab a drink, and settle in. Doors open early. Music starts at 7.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
Funky Fridays Vol.8 - Not My Sister, K-otik, Kimba Jade, Stolen Tuesday, We're back for another night of house, techno, disco, bass & more at Moon with Funky Fridays Vol. 8!
Created to revive and celebrate the underground electronic scene, Funky Friday is more than just a night out, it’s a space for music lovers, dancers, and creatives to connect through rhythm and energy. Each edition features a rotating lineup of talented DJs, both local and international, ensuring a fresh and unique experience every time.
Come with an open mind and leave with tired feet.
This edition features legends Stolen Tuesday, Kimba Jade, K-otik and Not My Sister
$10 presales available now via UTR, or $15 on the door.
Times:
9-10 Not My Sister
10-11 Stolen Tuesday
11-12 Kimba Jade
12.1 K-otik
1-2 Not My Sister
-------------------------------------------------------
NOT MY SISTER
Hailing from Italy and rooted in Wellington’s vibrant scene, Not My Sister burst onto the scene in 2024 and has quickly become one of Aotearoa’s most exciting rising DJs. In such a short time, she’s already lit up dance floors across the country, playing at festivals like Twisted Frequency, CubaDupa, Taniwha’s Den, and supporting international acts such as KC Lights.
She also holds a radio residency at Wellington’s iconic Radio Active, where her show dives deep into the world of house and underground sounds.
Heavily influenced by funk and disco, and driven by a love for vocals and all things groovy, Not My Sister delivers high-energy, uplifting sets full of passion and playfulness. She weaves together melodic basslines with soulful, tech, garage, and deep house elements, always keeping the crowd moving.
SoundCloud: www.soundcloud.com/not-my-sister
Facebook:
www.facebook.com/notmysista

Instagram: www.instagram.com/not.my.sister
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@not.my.sister
STOLEN TUESDAY
Stolen Tuesday started life as a house music club dancer in the UK 20 years
ago, and has spent the last 10 years DJing at Wanaka and Queenstown's best
establishments, bringing her unique energy to create magical dance floors.
Since residing in New Zealand, she has previously split her time living between Great Barrier island and Wanaka, spreading the love through her weekly radio shows on Wanaka Beats and Aotea FM. Not shy of the underground scene, you will also find Stolen Tuesday getting down off grid and playing at bush doofs.
Best known for dropping all things bassy which exude an element of fun matching her own high energy, guest appearances on Base FM (Auckland) alongside well known Auckland DJs have helped showcase her enthusiasm and talent.
With a style ranging from glitter box style disco and funk, to dirty after hours Tech House and all things in between, including the occasional breaks/ghetto funk set, it’s no surprise that She has been a regular on the South Island festival scene where she has been happy warming up or playing after notable international artists. She has also played at Otherlanz and remotely at Great Barrier Island’s new Wingman festival.
Stolen Tuesday now resides in Wellington and whilst fairly new to the city has already played several times for both the 'Open air' brand at Carlucci land and with Wellington renowned 'Little monsters' notably shaking things up at this year's Newtown festival.
Although not one to take herself too seriously, Stolen Tuesday knows it all comes down to the music and having a good time!
Soundcloud: https://soundcloud.com/user-720686581
KIMBA JADE
Kimba Jade has been lighting up dancefloors for over 20 years with her signature blend of deep, funky, and globally inspired grooves. A master of musical journeying, she weaves bass-driven beats with tribal, melodic, and soulful sounds that move both body and spirit.
With a deep passion for Ecstatic Dance and the power of music to connect and uplift, Kimba brings that same vibrant energy into clubs and bars. Her sets fuse Tech House, Indie Dance, and Techno, blending driving rhythms with emotive melodies to create an atmosphere that’s both immersive and electric.
After years in Sydney and Auckland’s underground scenes, Kimba now calls Wellington home—bringing her unique mix of rhythm, soul, and bass to local dance floors.
Soundcloud: https://soundcloud.com/kimba
Facebook:
www.facebook.com/kimbadj/

Instangram: @djkimbajade
K-OTIK
Based in Wellington, New Zealand, K-otiK is a multi-genre DJ. His sound spans house, tech house, bass house, and techno — laced with heavy vocals and thumping basslines. With a cheeky stage presence and high energy behind the decks, K-otiK takes crowds on journeys that balance careful curation with unexpected twists. A regular on RadioActive FM and part of Wellington’s Open Air collective, he’s built a reputation for spreading joy through music. For K-otiK, music does not discriminate — it’s the universal language that brings people together.
Instangram: @dj_kotik_nz

description:
Vocal Blokes NOT Over the Hill and Still Singing, Wellington Vocal Blokes are NOT going to Greytown as we first thought– Older, Bolder, and Still Singing, but we are having a workshop and concert in Wellington.
An afternoon of music, laughter, and good company on Saturday, 22 November.
Our end-of-year concert is bigger and better. This time we’re adding a singing workshop led by our young and inspiring music director, Paddy Shanahan, before rounding the day off with the 4:00 pm concert NOT Over the Hill and Still Singing at the Old Salvation Army Hall, 209 The Parade, Island Bay.
Workshop: Singing Together (2:00 – 3:30 pm)
Once you’ve warmed up, it’s time to build harmony. Drawing inspiration from Jacob Collier’s version of Coldplay’s Fix You, Paddy will guide the group in shaping simple “oohs” into rich, layered sound. It’s less about perfection and more about listening, blending, and feeling the joy of shared music.
You can take part in this workshop, and join the Vocal Blokes at the afternoon concert — a true “community choir” moment.
Workshops cost just $15 or $25 for both workshop and concert.
NOT Over the Hill and Still Singing is a playful nod to our cancelled journey from Wellington to Greytown and to the fact that most of us are, well, not exactly in our twenties anymore.
But that’s the beauty of it — we sing for the sheer love of it. Expect songs about travel, friendship, and meeting new people, all delivered with our trademark mix of heart and humour.
We love performing for audiences because you listen, laugh, and sing along in all the right places. There’s something special about our venue — the warmth, the curiosity, the way the hall fills with energy before the first note.
Come Join the Chorus. So, whether you’re a shower-soloist, a closet crooner, or simply someone who enjoys good company and great music, come spend the day with us. Bring your voice, bring a friend, and rediscover how good it feels to sing together. Workshop 2:00 pm. Concert at 4:00 pm.
label: Music & Concerts

description:
L-Side NZ Debut Tour (WLG), We are stoked to present one of DnB’s best for his first ever show in the capital, the one and only L-Side!
Hailing from São Paulo, Brazil, L-Side has been a mainstay in the drum and bass scene worldwide with releases on labels such as V Recordings, Hospital Records, The North Quarter and many, many more. Developing his own unique style, L-Side is known for his contrast of upfront and aggressive rolling basslines, merged with funk and soul samples and vocals, which has forged a beautifully fresh take on drum and bass.
On support we have some heavy hitters from our endlessly talented Wellington community: Pearly, Echotek, Edii & Gusty as well as Rhythm Output’s very own Gos & Fe joined by Timmy P.
Event runs 11pm-3am

description:
Journey into Om, Yoga without mantra is a bit like cake without the icing !
By now we all know that everything essentially is vibration. Yoga knew this 1000's of years ago, that is why sound is the first tool of Yoga.
Learn about the pranavah mantra ( om) as it relates to your Yoga Practice.
In this 2-hour workshop, you will understand the traditional and respectful way to use this sacred sound: both theoretically and practically.
We will draw on the ancient texts of Yoga as our point of reference.
The use of a mantra is a powerful tool for healing, emotional and mental clarity, and spiritual development. You will be guided in a variety of accessible practices to experience the effects of this mantra.
This workshop can open new dimensions to your practice, or it is also a great way to start on the Yoga Journey.
I teach from the Viniyoga tradition of Krishnamacharya, where mantra is used as an integral aspect of Yoga, whether it be asana, pranayama, or meditation.
This workshop will be nourishing to all the dimensions -heart, mind, and body.
You do not need to be a singer at all-just be willing to have a go at making a sound, even in your head if out loud feels too weird.
label: Religion & Spirituality

description:
Masterclass - Awaken Your Soul Purpose, Masterclass - Awaken Your Soul Purpose
When we feel safe in our bodies, everything becomes clearer.
We can finally soften out of survival mode…
We hear our intuition again…
And we reconnect with the quiet, wise voice of our Soul.

So many women are trying to “find their purpose” from a dysregulated nervous system - overthinking, pushing, striving - when what their soul is really asking for is safety, presence, and space.

Because when your nervous system settles, your truth rises.

This masterclass is a gentle nervous system reset designed to help you come home to yourself and open to your Soul’s guidance.

You will be guided through:
✨ Soft, grounding movement to release tension and bring you back into your body
✨ A deeply calming meditation with soul-evoking prompts to invite clarity and insight
✨ A powerful base-chakra somatic practice to create a felt sense of safety and belonging
✨ Space to receive the subtle visions, feelings and knowings that reveal your Soul Purpose

From this place of embodied safety, your inner wisdom can finally speak.

This is not about forcing answers.

It’s about creating the conditions where they naturally arise.

This masterclass will help you connect with what is within you, quietly waiting to be heard - so you can live, choose and create in alignment with who you truly are.

As we step into the Year of the Fire Horse, this is your invitation to release what no longer serves you and move forward with clarity, passion and purposeful alignment 🐎🔥

Your attendance qualifies you for a special offer regarding the life-changing, Soul Purpose Expansion retreat in Ubud, Bali later this year.

This Masterclass is free to attend.

We do request that you indicate you are attending on this event and please click on the ticket link below to submit your contact details, to ensure you receive the link to the live event (and the recording if you can't make the live event).

Susan and I are looking forward to facilitating this masterclass for you.

Namaste

description:
Banks Arcade // Wellington // NU - Zealand Tour // With Ocean Grove & No Life, Banks Arcade // Wellington // NU - Zealand Tour // With Ocean Grove & No Life
BANKS ARCADE ANNOUNCE TRIUMPHANT RETURN TO NEW ZEALAND FOR TWO EXPLOSIVE SHOWS

Auckland, New Zealand — 19th December 2025 — After a monumental few years sharing stages with global heavyweights including Highly Suspect, Northlane, and The Plot In You, and selling out headline shows across Australia and New Zealand, Banks Arcade have announced their highly anticipated return home this December. The genre-bending powerhouse will headline two unmissable nights on December 19 and December 20, marking a powerful homecoming to New Zealand stages.

Fans can expect an electrifying set packed with the band's fierce catalogue of anthems, showcasing the explosive live energy that has cemented them as one of the fastest-rising forces in heavy music.

Joining the lineup are Melbourne's genre-defying trailblazers Ocean Grove, whose wild live shows have earned them a reputation as one of Australia's most unpredictable acts. Also on board are No Life, New Zealand's rising heavy hitters, ready to set the tone with their unrelenting intensity.

Quote from the band

"Coming back to New Zealand to play these shows feels like a full-circle moment for us. This is where it all began, and we can't wait to bring the energy home and share the stage with some of our closest friends," says Banks Arcade.

🎟 Tickets are on sale now at www.banksarcade.com — get in quick, these will move fast.

description:
Made to bloom, exhibition by Josefine Gronkrans, We have a solo exhibition coming up from one of our ceramic artists Josefine Gronkrans
The opening night is 14th October, Tuesday at 5:30 pm
Vincents gallery : Willis St Village
5/148 Willis St
Wellington
14th October - 4th November
Pencil the date.
Nau mai haere mai.
Josefine Gronkrans bio
I'm a ceramicists from a rural part of Wellington who take inspiration from the beauty of Creation. I moved to beautiful Aotearoa from Sweden a few years ago, and my journey with ceramics started in 2023 and I've been elbows deep in clay ever since. I use flora found all over the world, and outside my doorsteps, to shape my artwork. My ceramic pieces are all hand made and no piece will be the other alike. I mainly throw a shape on the wheel and then let it tell me what it wants to become.
This exhibition is guided by Ecclesiastes 3:1-11. I think life is beautiful in it's times of joy, but also in the times of struggle, where we can grow and learn.
A Time for Everything
There is a time for everything,
and a season for every activity under the heavens:
a time to be born and a time to die,
a time to plant and a time to uproot,
a time to kill and a time to heal,
a time to tear down and a time to build,
a time to weep and a time to laugh,
a time to mourn and a time to dance,
a time to scatter stones and a time to gather them,
a time to embrace and a time to refrain from embracing,
a time to search and a time to give up,
a time to keep and a time to throw away,
a time to tear and a time to mend,
a time to be silent and a time to speak,
a time to love and a time to hate,
a time for war and a time for peace.
What do workers gain from their toil? I have seen the burden God has laid on the human race. He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the human heart; yet no one can fathom what God has done from beginning to end.
label: Arts & Theatre
